 107# helper routine for counting number of paramerters in model
 108def count_parameters(model):
 109    return sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters() if p.requires_grad)
 110
 111
 112# convenience function for logging weights and gradients
 113def log_weights_and_grads(model, iters=1):
 114    root_path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "weights_and_grads")
 115
 116    weights_and_grads_fname = os.path.join(root_path, f"weights_and_grads_step{iters:03d}.tar")
 117    print(weights_and_grads_fname)
 118
 119    weights_dict = {k: v for k, v in model.named_parameters()}
 120    grad_dict = {k: v.grad for k, v in model.named_parameters()}
 121
 122    store_dict = {"iteration": iters, "grads": grad_dict, "weights": weights_dict}
 123    torch.save(store_dict, weights_and_grads_fname)
 124
 125
 126# rolls out the FNO and compares to the classical solver
 127def gaussian_bump_initial_condition(solver):
 128    """
 129    Generate a static 'lifted height' initial condition.
 130    Gaussian bump in geopotential height centered at 45N, 180E.
 131    Zero vorticity and zero divergence.
 132    """
 133    device = solver.lap.device
 134    ctype = torch.complex128 if solver.lap.dtype == torch.float64 else torch.complex64
 135    
 136    # Grid coordinates
 137    # lats: (nlat,), lons: (nlon,)
 138    lats = solver.lats.squeeze()
 139    lons = solver.lons.squeeze()
 140    Lats, Lons = torch.meshgrid(lats, lons, indexing='ij')
 141    
 142    # Bump parameters
 143    lat0 = 45.0 * math.pi / 180.0
 144    lon0 = math.pi # 180 degrees
 145    # Use a finite radius for the Cosine Bell to ensure exact zero support
 146    radius_bump = 40.0 * math.pi / 180.0 
 147    amp = solver.hamp * 2.0 # Reduced amplitude 
 148    
 149    # Distance (Great circle central angle)
 150    central_angle = torch.acos(
 151        torch.clamp(
 152            torch.sin(Lats) * math.sin(lat0) + 
 153            torch.cos(Lats) * math.cos(lat0) * torch.cos(Lons - lon0), 
 154            -1.0, 1.0
 155        )
 156    )
 157    dist = central_angle
 158    
 159    # Cosine Bell: 0.5 * A * (1 + cos(pi * r / R)) for r < R
 160    h_perturbation = torch.zeros_like(dist)
 161    mask = dist < radius_bump
 162    h_perturbation[mask] = 0.5 * amp * (1.0 + torch.cos(math.pi * dist[mask] / radius_bump))
 163    
 164    h_grid = solver.havg + h_perturbation
 165    
 166    # Scale by gravity (solver expects geopotential)
 167    phi_grid = solver.gravity * h_grid
 168    
 169    # Convert to spectral
 170    phi_spec = solver.grid2spec(phi_grid)
 171    
 172    # Full state: [Phi, Vort, Div]
 173    uspec = torch.zeros(3, solver.lmax, solver.mmax, dtype=ctype, device=device)
 174    uspec[0] = phi_spec
 175    # Vorticity (1) and Divergence (2) remain zero
 176    
 177    return uspec
 178
 179def autoregressive_inference(
 180    model,
 181    dataset,
 182    loss_fn,
 183    metrics_fns,
 184    path_root,
 185    nsteps,
 186    autoreg_steps=10,
 187    nskip=1,
 188    plot_channel=0,
 189    nics=10,
 190    device=torch.device("cpu"),
 191    run_tag=None,
 192    n_history=1,  # Number of past timesteps model expects (1=single-step, 3=AB3-like)
 193    precomputed_dataset=None,
 194):
 195
 196    model.eval()
 197
 198    if nics <= 0:
 199        print("Skipping autoregressive inference (nics=0)")
 200        return torch.tensor([]), {}, [], []
 201
 202    # make output
 203    if not os.path.isdir(path_root):
 204        os.makedirs(path_root, exist_ok=True)
 205
 206    # accumulation buffers for losses, metrics and runtimes
 207    losses = torch.zeros(nics, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
 208    metrics = {}
 209    for metric in metrics_fns:
 210        metrics[metric] = torch.zeros(nics, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
 211    model_times = torch.zeros(nics, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
 212    solver_times = torch.zeros(nics, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
 213
 214    # accumulation buffers for the power spectrum
 215    prd_mean_coeffs = []
 216    ref_mean_coeffs = []
 217
 218    # Note: We need to accumulate fields for NetCDF saving
 219    # shape: (nics, steps, C, lat, lon)
 220    all_preds = []
 221    all_truth = []
 222    
 223    # New buffers for u and v components
 224    all_preds_u = []
 225    all_preds_v = []
 226    all_truth_u = []
 227    all_truth_v = []
 228    
 229    for iic in range(nics):
 230        # Use Galewsky IC for first sample, Gaussian Bump for second, random for rest
 231        use_precomputed_truth = False
 232        states_chunk = None
 233
 234        if iic == 0:
 235            ic = dataset.solver.galewsky_initial_condition()
 236        elif iic == 1:
 237            ic = gaussian_bump_initial_condition(dataset.solver)
 238        else:
 239            if precomputed_dataset is not None:
 240                # Use precomputed trajectory
 241                use_precomputed_truth = True
 242                
 243                # Sample random trajectory and start time
 244                # Ensure we have enough future steps: t_start + autoreg_steps <= T_total - 1
 245                # So max t_start = T_total - 1 - autoreg_steps
 246                max_start = precomputed_dataset.T_total - 1 - autoreg_steps
 247                
 248                # If window is too large for trajectory, this might fail, but T_total should be large
 249                if max_start < 0:
 250                     raise ValueError(f"Trajectory length {precomputed_dataset.T_total} too short for validation rollout {autoreg_steps}")
 251                
 252                traj_idx = torch.randint(0, precomputed_dataset.N, (1,)).item()
 253                t_idx = torch.randint(0, max_start + 1, (1,)).item()
 254                
 255                # Load chunk: [t_idx, t_idx + autoreg_steps + 1) -> length autoreg_steps + 1
 256                # states are (N, T, C, H, W)
 257                # We need to handle potential retries here too if Zarr is flaky, but dataset class has it.
 258                # However we are accessing .states directly here.
 259                # Let's trust Zarr combined with the earlier retry patch or just do a simple try/loop if needed.
 260                # Since we are in validation, a crash is annoying but less critical than training loop. 
 261                # We'll access via the dataset object if possible or just direct slice. 
 262                # Direct slice is easiest given we need a custom window length.
 263                
 264                # shape: (autoreg_steps + 1, C, H, W)
 265                states_chunk = torch.from_numpy(precomputed_dataset.states[traj_idx, t_idx : t_idx + autoreg_steps + 1])
 266                states_chunk = states_chunk.float().to(device)
 267                
 268                # Initial state (step 0)
 269                state0_grid = states_chunk[0] # (C, H, W)
 270                
 271                # Convert to spectral IC for model input compatibility (if model needs spectral)
 272                # But here we just need Grid state for normalization
 273                # We do need 'ic' (spectral) if we were running solver, but we aren't.
 274                # However, consistent with below, we might want 'ic' for some reason? 
 275                # Actually, 'uspec' is derived from 'ic'. 'uspec' is used for solver stepping.
 276                # We won't step solver, so we don't strictly need 'ic' or 'uspec' for truth evolution.
 277                # But we do need 'ic' if we want to calculate initial U/V using solver methods (grid2spec)
 278                ic = dataset.solver.grid2spec(state0_grid)
 279                
 280            else:
 281                ic = dataset.solver.random_initial_condition(mach=0.2)
 282
 283        inp_mean = dataset.inp_mean
 284        inp_var = dataset.inp_var
 285
 286        # Initial Condition (step 0) - single normalized state (C, H, W)
 287        if use_precomputed_truth:
 288             # state0_grid already extracted
 289             pass
 290        else:
 291             state0_grid = dataset.solver.spec2grid(ic)
 292             
 293        state0 = (state0_grid - inp_mean) / torch.sqrt(inp_var)
 294        state0 = state0.unsqueeze(0)  # (1, C, H, W)
 295        
 296        if not use_precomputed_truth:
 297            uspec = ic.clone()
 298
 299        # Storage for this IC
 300        ic_preds = [state0[0].detach().cpu()]
 301        ic_truth = [state0[0].detach().cpu()]
 302        
 303        # Calculate initial u/v for prediction (from ground truth IC)
 304        # dataset.solver.getuv expects spectral vorticity and divergence
 305        # ic is full spectral state (height, vorticity, divergence)
 306        uv_ic = dataset.solver.getuv(ic[1:]) # (2, lat, lon)
 307        ic_preds_u = [uv_ic[0].detach().cpu()]
 308        ic_preds_v = [uv_ic[1].detach().cpu()]
 309        ic_truth_u = [uv_ic[0].detach().cpu()]
 310        ic_truth_v = [uv_ic[1].detach().cpu()]
 311
 312        # add IC to power spectrum series
 313        prd_coeffs = [dataset.sht(state0[0, plot_channel]).detach().cpu().clone()]
 314        ref_coeffs = [prd_coeffs[0].clone()]
 315
 316        # Initialize history buffer for multi-step models
 317        # For n_history=3: buffer = [state0, state0, state0] initially (like AB3's Forward Euler)
 318        # Each state is (1, C, H, W), and they get stacked to (1, n_history*C, H, W)
 319        if n_history > 1:
 320            history_buffer = [state0.clone() for _ in range(n_history)]
 321        else:
 322            # For 1-step, we rely on 'prd' variable
 323            pass # history_buffer = None
 324
 325        # ML model autoregressive rollout
 326        start_time = time.time()
 327        # Initialize prd for single-step logic
 328        prd = state0 
 329        
 330        for i in range(1, autoreg_steps + 1):
 331            # Build model input based on n_history
 332            if n_history > 1:
 333                # Stack history: [oldest, ..., newest] -> (1, n_history*C, H, W)
 334                model_input = torch.cat(history_buffer, dim=1)
 335            else:
 336                # Single-step: input is just the previous prediction
 337                model_input = prd
 338            
 339            # Evaluate the ML model
 340            prd = model(model_input)  # Output: (1, C, H, W)
 341            
 342            # Update history buffer (sliding window)
 343            if n_history > 1:
 344                history_buffer = history_buffer[1:] + [prd.clone()]
 345            
 346            # Store prediction (only the output C channels, not the stacked input)
 347            ic_preds.append(prd[0].detach().cpu())
 348            prd_coeffs.append(dataset.sht(prd[0, plot_channel]).detach().cpu().clone())
 349            
 350            # Calculate and store u/v for prediction
 351            # 1. Denormalize to get physical grid values
 352            prd_phys = prd[0] * torch.sqrt(inp_var) + inp_mean
 353            # 2. Convert to spectral to get vorticity/divergence coeffs
 354            prd_spec = dataset.solver.grid2spec(prd_phys)
 355            # 3. Calculate u/v from vorticity/divergence (channels 1 and 2)
 356            uv_prd = dataset.solver.getuv(prd_spec[1:])
 357            ic_preds_u.append(uv_prd[0].detach().cpu())
 358            ic_preds_v.append(uv_prd[1].detach().cpu())
 359
 360        model_times[iic] = time.time() - start_time
 361
 362        # classical model (or precomputed truth)
 363        start_time = time.time()
 364        
 365        if use_precomputed_truth:
 366            # Use precomputed trajectory slice
 367            # states_chunk has shape (steps+1, C, H, W)
 368            # We already used index 0 for IC. Now iterate 1..steps
 369            for i in range(1, autoreg_steps + 1):
 370                # Get grid state directly
 371                ref_phys = states_chunk[i] # (C, H, W)
 372                
 373                # Normalize for metrics/loss
 374                ref = (ref_phys - inp_mean) / torch.sqrt(inp_var)
 375                ic_truth.append(ref.detach().cpu())
 376                
 377                ref_coeffs.append(dataset.sht(ref[plot_channel]).detach().cpu().clone())
 378                
 379                # Calculate U/V from physical grid state
 380                # Need consistent usage of solver utils
 381                ref_spec = dataset.solver.grid2spec(ref_phys)
 382                uv_ref = dataset.solver.getuv(ref_spec[1:])
 383                ic_truth_u.append(uv_ref[0].detach().cpu())
 384                ic_truth_v.append(uv_ref[1].detach().cpu())
 385                
 386        else:
 387            # Run solver on-the-fly
 388            for i in range(1, autoreg_steps + 1):
 389                # advance classical model
 390                uspec = dataset.solver.timestep(uspec, nsteps)
 391                ref = (dataset.solver.spec2grid(uspec) - inp_mean) / torch.sqrt(inp_var)
 392                
 393                # Store truth
 394                ic_truth.append(ref.detach().cpu())
 395
 396                ref_coeffs.append(dataset.sht(ref[plot_channel]).detach().cpu().clone())
 397                
 398                # Calculate and store u/v for truth
 399                # uspec already has physical spectral coefficients
 400                uv_ref = dataset.solver.getuv(uspec[1:])
 401                ic_truth_u.append(uv_ref[0].detach().cpu())
 402                ic_truth_v.append(uv_ref[1].detach().cpu())
 403
 404        solver_times[iic] = time.time() - start_time
 405        
 406        # Append to main storage
 407        all_preds.append(torch.stack(ic_preds)) # (steps, C, H, W)
 408        all_truth.append(torch.stack(ic_truth))
 409        all_preds_u.append(torch.stack(ic_preds_u))
 410        all_preds_v.append(torch.stack(ic_preds_v))
 411        all_truth_u.append(torch.stack(ic_truth_u))
 412        all_truth_v.append(torch.stack(ic_truth_v))
 413
 414        # compute power spectrum and add it to the buffers
 415        prd_mean_coeffs.append(torch.stack(prd_coeffs, 0))
 416        ref_mean_coeffs.append(torch.stack(ref_coeffs, 0))
 417
 418        # Compute metrics on the LAST step (as was done originally)
 419        # ref re-calculated/extracted for consistency with original loss logic
 420        ref_last = ic_truth[-1].to(device).unsqueeze(0) 
 421        prd_last = ic_preds[-1].to(device).unsqueeze(0)
 422        
 423        losses[iic] = loss_fn(prd_last, ref_last)
 424        for metric in metrics_fns:
 425            metric_buff = metrics[metric]
 426            metric_fn = metrics_fns[metric]
 427            metric_buff[iic] = metric_fn(prd_last, ref_last)
 428
 429    # compute the averaged powerspectra 
 430    with torch.no_grad():
 431        prd_mean_coeffs = torch.stack(prd_mean_coeffs, dim=0).abs().pow(2).mean(dim=0)
 432        ref_mean_coeffs = torch.stack(ref_mean_coeffs, dim=0).abs().pow(2).mean(dim=0)
 433
 434        prd_mean_coeffs[..., 1:] *= 2.0
 435        ref_mean_coeffs[..., 1:] *= 2.0
 436        prd_mean_ps = prd_mean_coeffs.sum(dim=-1).contiguous()
 437        ref_mean_ps = ref_mean_coeffs.sum(dim=-1).contiguous()
 438
 439    # SAVE NETCDF
 440    import xarray as xr
 441    
 442    # Stack all data: (nics, steps, C, H, W)
 443    preds_tensor = torch.stack(all_preds).numpy()
 444    truth_tensor = torch.stack(all_truth).numpy()
 445    
 446    # Stack UV data: (nics, steps, H, W) -> (nics, steps, 1, H, W)
 447    preds_u = torch.stack(all_preds_u).unsqueeze(2).numpy()
 448    preds_v = torch.stack(all_preds_v).unsqueeze(2).numpy()
 449    truth_u = torch.stack(all_truth_u).unsqueeze(2).numpy()
 450    truth_v = torch.stack(all_truth_v).unsqueeze(2).numpy()
 451    
 452    # Concatenate along channel dimension (axis 2)
 453    # Resulting shape: (nics, steps, 5, H, W)
 454    preds_combined = np.concatenate([preds_tensor, preds_u, preds_v], axis=2)
 455    truth_combined = np.concatenate([truth_tensor, truth_u, truth_v], axis=2)
 456    
 457    # Define coords
 458    times = np.arange(autoreg_steps + 1)
 459    samples = np.arange(nics)
 460    # chans = np.arange(dataset.nlat) if preds_tensor.shape[2] == dataset.nlat else np.arange(preds_tensor.shape[2]) # Usually 3 chans
 461    chans = ["geopotential_height", "vorticity", "divergence", "u", "v"]
 462    lats = np.linspace(-90, 90, dataset.nlat) # Approx, ideally use dataset.grid
 463    lons = np.linspace(0, 360, dataset.nlon, endpoint=False)
 464    
 465    ds = xr.Dataset(
 466        data_vars={
 467            "prediction": (("sample", "time", "channel", "lat", "lon"), preds_combined),
 468            "truth": (("sample", "time", "channel", "lat", "lon"), truth_combined),
 469        },
 470        coords={
 471            "sample": samples,
 472            "time": times,
 473            "channel": chans,
 474            "lat": lats,
 475            "lon": lons,
 476        }
 477    )
 478    
 479    scratch_dir = f"/glade/derecho/scratch/{os.environ['USER']}/TH_SWE_output"
 480    if not os.path.exists(scratch_dir):
 481        os.makedirs(scratch_dir, exist_ok=True)
 482    
 483    # Extract model name from path_root (.../model_name/figures)
 484    model_name = os.path.basename(os.path.dirname(path_root))
 485    # Build filename with optional run_tag
 486    if run_tag:
 487        save_path = os.path.join(scratch_dir, f"{model_name}_{run_tag}_validation_steps{autoreg_steps}.nc")
 488    else:
 489        save_path = os.path.join(scratch_dir, f"{model_name}_validation_steps{autoreg_steps}.nc")
 490    print(f"Saving NetCDF to {save_path}...")
 491    ds.to_netcdf(save_path)
 492    print(f"Save complete. Output file is located at: {save_path}")
 493
 494    return losses, metrics, model_times, solver_times
 495
 496
 497# training function
 498def train_model(
 499    model,
 500    dataloader,
 501    loss_fn,
 502    metrics_fns,
 503    optimizer,
 504    gscaler,
 505    scheduler=None,
 506    nepochs=20,
 507    nfuture=0,
 508    num_examples=256,
 509    num_valid=0,
 510    amp_mode="none",
 511    log_grads=0,
 512    logging=True,
 513    device=torch.device("cpu"),
 514    n_history=1,  # Number of past timesteps model expects
 515    noise_injection_sigma=0.0,
 516    noise_injection_rate=0.0,
 517    plot_callback=None,
 518    scratch_dir=None,
 519    time_diff2_lambda=0.0,
 520):
 521   
 522    train_start = time.time()
 523
 524    # set AMP type
 525    amp_dtype = torch.float32
 526    if amp_mode == "fp16":
 527        amp_dtype = torch.float16
 528    elif amp_mode == "bf16":
 529        amp_dtype = torch.bfloat16
 530
 531    # count iterations
 532    iters = 0
 533    valid_loss = 0.0 # Initialize valid_loss to prevent UnboundLocalError if nepochs=0
 534    best_valid_loss = float('inf')
 535
 536    for epoch in range(nepochs):
 537
 538        # time each epoch
 539        epoch_start = time.time()
 540
 541        if hasattr(dataloader.dataset, 'set_initial_condition'):
 542            dataloader.dataset.set_initial_condition("random")
 543        if hasattr(dataloader.dataset, 'set_num_examples'):
 544            dataloader.dataset.set_num_examples(num_examples)
 545
 546        # get the solver for its convenience functions
 547        solver = getattr(dataloader.dataset, 'solver', None)
 548
 549        # do the training
 550        accumulated_loss = 0
 551        accumulated_l2_loss = 0
 552        accumulated_lap_loss = 0
 553        accumulated_cons_loss = 0
 554        accumulated_ccc_loss = 0
 555        accumulated_spectral_loss = 0
 556        accumulated_gradient_loss = 0
 557        accumulated_spectral_pv_loss = 0
 558        accumulated_spectral_pv_loss = 0
 559        accumulated_mass_loss = 0
 560        accumulated_time_diff2_loss = 0
 561        model.train()
 562
 563        for batch_idx, (inp, tar) in enumerate(dataloader):
 564
 565            inp, tar = inp.to(device), tar.to(device)
 566
 567            # --- Noise Injection Logic ---
 568            if noise_injection_sigma > 0 and noise_injection_rate > 0 and inp.size(0) >= 2:
 569                batch_size = inp.size(0)
 570                half_batch = batch_size // 2
 571                
 572                # Iterate over the first half of the batch
 573                for i in range(half_batch):
 574                    # With probability `noise_injection_rate`, duplicate sample `i` to `i + half_batch`
 575                    if torch.rand(1).item() < noise_injection_rate:
 576                        target_idx = i + half_batch
 577                        
 578                        # Duplicate input
 579                        inp[target_idx] = inp[i].clone()
 580                        
 581                        # Duplicate target (Ground truth for noisy input is clean target)
 582                        if tar.dim() == 5: # Trajectory (B, T, C, H, W)
 583                             tar[target_idx] = tar[i].clone()
 584                        else: # Single step (B, C, H, W)
 585                             tar[target_idx] = tar[i].clone()
 586                        
 587                        # Add noise to the duplicate input
 588                        noise = torch.randn_like(inp[target_idx]) * noise_injection_sigma
 589                        inp[target_idx] += noise
 590            # -----------------------------
 591
 592            with torch.autocast(device_type="cuda", dtype=amp_dtype, enabled=(amp_mode != "none")):
 593
 594                # Check if tar is a trajectory (5D: B, T, C, H, W) or single target (4D: B, C, H, W)
 595                if tar.dim() == 5:
 596                    # Multi-step trajectory: accumulate loss at each step
 597                    loss = 0
 598                    num_steps = tar.shape[1]
 599                    prev_prev_state = None
 600                    
 601                    if batch_idx == 0 and epoch == 0:
 602                        print(f"DEBUG: Autoregressive training running. tar shape: {tar.shape}, num_steps: {num_steps}, dataset: {dataloader.dataset.__class__.__name__}")
 603                    
 604                    if hasattr(loss_fn, 'reset_stats'):
 605                        loss_fn.reset_stats()
 606                    
 607                    # For multi-history: need to maintain history buffer
 608                    if n_history > 1:
 609                        # inp shape: (B, n_history*C, H, W), extract per-timestep chunks
 610                        B, total_C, H, W = inp.shape
 611                        physical_chans = total_C // n_history  # 3 for SWE
 612                        # Initialize history buffer with input timesteps
 613                        history_buffer = [inp[:, i*physical_chans:(i+1)*physical_chans] for i in range(n_history)]
 614                        
 615                        for step in range(num_steps):
 616                            model_input = torch.cat(history_buffer, dim=1)
 617                            prd = model(model_input)  # Output: (B, C, H, W)
 618                            step_target = tar[:, step]
 619                            
 620                            # Identify prev_target, prev_prev_target for loss
 621                            prev_target = None
 622                            prev_prev_target = None
 623                            
 624                            if step == 0:
 625                                # For step 0, prev_target is last frame of input
 626                                prev_target = history_buffer[-1]
 627                                if n_history > 1:
 628                                     # prev_prev is second to last
 629                                     prev_prev_target = history_buffer[-2]
 630                            else:
 631                                prev_target = tar[:, step-1]
 632                                if step == 1:
 633                                    prev_prev_target = history_buffer[-1]
 634                                else:
 635                                    prev_prev_target = tar[:, step-2]
 636                            
 637                            # For step 0, prev_state is the last input frame (from history or inp)
 638                            # If step > 0, prev_state is the previous prediction
 639                            if step == 0:
 640                                # history_buffer[-1] is the most recent frame
 641                                prev_state = history_buffer[-1]
 642                                if n_history > 1:
 643                                    prev_prev_state = history_buffer[-2]
 644                            else:
 645                                prev_state = history_buffer[-1] 
 646                                if n_history > 1:
 647                                    prev_prev_state = history_buffer[-2]
 648                                else:
 649                                    # For n_history=1, we need to manually track prev_prev if available
 650                                    # But in this branch (n_history>1) we have history_buffer.
 651                                    pass
 652                            
 653                            # Let's simplify: pass model_input (which contains history)
 654                            # CombinedLoss can extract the last frame.
 655                            loss = loss + loss_fn(prd, step_target, 
 656                                                prev_state=prev_state, 
 657                                                prev_prev_state=prev_prev_state,
 658                                                prev_target=prev_target,
 659                                                prev_prev_target=prev_prev_target,
 660                                                step=step)
 661                            
 662                            # Update prev_prev_state for NEXT step (it becomes prev_state of next step)
 663                            if n_history > 1:
 664                                prev_prev_state = history_buffer[-1]
 665                            else:
 666                                prev_prev_state = model_input
 667
 668                            # Update history buffer (sliding window)
 669                            history_buffer = history_buffer[1:] + [prd]
 670                    else:
 671                        # Single-history: original behavior
 672                        prd = inp
 673                        prev_target = None
 674                        prev_prev_target = None
 675                        
 676                        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'reset_stats'):
 677                            loss_fn.reset_stats()
 678                            
 679                        for step in range(num_steps):
 680                            model_input = prd # Save previous state
 681                            
 682                            # Update targets
 683                            step_target = tar[:, step]
 684                            if step == 0:
 685                                prev_target = inp
 686                                prev_prev_target = None # Can't know t-2 easily without history
 687                            else:
 688                                prev_target = tar[:, step-1]
 689                                if step == 1:
 690                                    prev_prev_target = inp
 691                                else:
 692                                    prev_prev_target = tar[:, step-2]
 693
 694                            prd = model(prd)
 695                            
 696                            if step == 0:
 697                                prev_prev_state = None
 698                            else:
 699                                prev_prev_state = model_input
 700                            
 701                            loss = loss + loss_fn(prd, step_target, 
 702                                                prev_state=model_input, 
 703                                                prev_prev_state=prev_prev_state,
 704                                                prev_target=prev_target,
 705                                                prev_prev_target=prev_prev_target,
 706                                                step=step)
 707                    
 708                    # Average over steps for consistent loss magnitude
 709                    loss = loss / num_steps
 710                else:
 711                    # Single-step target: original behavior with optional nfuture rollout
 712                    if n_history > 1 and nfuture > 0:
 713                        # Multi-history with rollout: need history buffer
 714                        B, total_C, H, W = inp.shape
 715                        physical_chans = total_C // n_history
 716                        history_buffer = [inp[:, i*physical_chans:(i+1)*physical_chans] for i in range(n_history)]
 717                        
 718                        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'reset_stats'):
 719                            loss_fn.reset_stats()
 720                            
 721                        for _ in range(nfuture + 1):  # +1 for initial prediction
 722                            model_input = torch.cat(history_buffer, dim=1)
 723                            prd = model(model_input)
 724                            history_buffer = history_buffer[1:] + [prd]
 725                        # So we pass the last input as prev_state logic?
 726                        # If nfuture > 0, we did multiple steps. The last prd is result of last model_input.
 727                        # For single-step loss with rollout (nfuture>0), we don't have intermediate targets in this branch
 728                        # But if we want time_diff2 loss on the FINAL step, we need u_{T-1} and u_{T-2}
 729                        # The loop above updates history_buffer.
 730                        # history_buffer[-2] would be u_{T-2} effectively?
 731                        # This branch is rarely used with time_diff2 but let's try to support it.
 732                        prev_prev = None
 733                        if nfuture >= 1 and n_history > 1:
 734                             prev_prev = history_buffer[-2] 
 735
 736                        loss = loss_fn(prd, tar, prev_state=model_input, prev_prev_state=prev_prev, step=nfuture)
 737                    else:
 738                        # Standard single-step or single-history
 739                        model_input = inp
 740                        prd = model(inp)
 741                        prev_prev_state = None
 742                        prev_state = model_input
 743                        
 744                        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'reset_stats'):
 745                            loss_fn.reset_stats()
 746                        
 747                        # Store initial state as prev_prev if nfuture >= 1
 748                        # Store initial state as prev_prev if nfuture >= 1
 749                        if nfuture >= 1:
 750                             prev_prev_state = prev_state # u_t
 751                             prev_state = prd # u_{t+1} (after first step)
 752                             
 753                             # Track ground truth history
 754                             # For step 0 (implicit above): prev_target=inp, target=tar[:,0]
 755                             # For step 1 (first iter below): prev_target=tar[:,0], etc.
 756                             pass
 757
 758                        for step in range(nfuture):
 759                            # logic: 
 760                            # step 0 (already done above): inp -> prd(t+1). prev=inp. prev_prev=None.
 761                            # step 1 (loop): model_input=prd(t+1). prd(t+2). prev=prd(t+1). prev_prev=inp.
 762                            
 763                            # We need to correctly identify prev_target and prev_prev_target from Ground Truth
 764                            # The 'tar' variable in this branch is (B, C, H, W) - single step target.
 765                            # Wait, 'tar' is single step. 'nfuture' means we rollout WITHOUT gradient supervision on intermediate steps?
 766                            # No, usually nfuture training involves multi-step loss?
 767                            # In this specific branch (else: single step target), 'tar' is just the immediate next step.
 768                            # So we CANNOT calculate diff2_gt effectively because we don't have t+2 ground truth.
 769                            # So this branch only supports diff2 loss if nfuture=0 (default).
 770                            pass
 771                            
 772                            if step > 0:
 773                                 prev_prev_state = prev_state
 774                                 prev_state = prd
 775
 776                            model_input = prd
 777                            prd = model(prd)
 778                        
 779                        loss = loss_fn(prd, tar, prev_state=model_input, prev_prev_state=prev_prev_state, prev_target=None, prev_prev_target=None, step=nfuture)
 780
 781            accumulated_loss += loss.item() * inp.size(0)
 782            
 783            # For trajectory generation, we accumulated raw components across rollout steps. Scale them back to step-averages.
 784            div = tar.shape[1] if tar.dim() == 5 else 1.0
 785            
 786            # Accumulate loss components if available
 787            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_l2_loss'):
 788                accumulated_l2_loss += (loss_fn.last_l2_loss / div) * inp.size(0)
 789            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_lap_loss'):
 790                accumulated_lap_loss += (loss_fn.last_lap_loss / div) * inp.size(0)
 791            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_cons_loss'):
 792                accumulated_cons_loss += (loss_fn.last_cons_loss / div) * inp.size(0)
 793            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_ccc_loss'):
 794                accumulated_ccc_loss += (loss_fn.last_ccc_loss / div) * inp.size(0)
 795            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_spectral_loss'):
 796                accumulated_spectral_loss += (loss_fn.last_spectral_loss / div) * inp.size(0)
 797            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_gradient_loss'):
 798                accumulated_gradient_loss += (loss_fn.last_gradient_loss / div) * inp.size(0)
 799            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_spectral_pv_loss'):
 800                accumulated_spectral_pv_loss += (loss_fn.last_spectral_pv_loss / div) * inp.size(0)
 801            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_mass_loss'):
 802                accumulated_mass_loss += (loss_fn.last_mass_loss / div) * inp.size(0)
 803            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_time_diff2_loss'):
 804                accumulated_time_diff2_loss += (loss_fn.last_time_diff2_loss / div) * inp.size(0)
 805
 806            optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
 807            gscaler.scale(loss).backward()
 808
 809            if log_grads and iters % log_grads == 0:
 810                log_weights_and_grads(model, iters=iters)
 811
 812            gscaler.step(optimizer)
 813            gscaler.update()
 814
 815            iters += 1
 816
 817        accumulated_loss = accumulated_loss / len(dataloader.dataset)
 818        # Average loss components and store for logging
 819        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_l2_loss'):
 820            loss_fn.last_l2_loss = accumulated_l2_loss / len(dataloader.dataset)
 821        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_lap_loss'):
 822            loss_fn.last_lap_loss = accumulated_lap_loss / len(dataloader.dataset)
 823        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_cons_loss'):
 824            loss_fn.last_cons_loss = accumulated_cons_loss / len(dataloader.dataset)
 825        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_ccc_loss'):
 826            loss_fn.last_ccc_loss = accumulated_ccc_loss / len(dataloader.dataset)
 827        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_spectral_loss'):
 828            loss_fn.last_spectral_loss = accumulated_spectral_loss / len(dataloader.dataset)
 829        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_gradient_loss'):
 830            loss_fn.last_gradient_loss = accumulated_gradient_loss / len(dataloader.dataset)
 831        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_spectral_pv_loss'):
 832            loss_fn.last_spectral_pv_loss = accumulated_spectral_pv_loss / len(dataloader.dataset)
 833        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_mass_loss'):
 834            loss_fn.last_mass_loss = accumulated_mass_loss / len(dataloader.dataset)
 835        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_time_diff2_loss'):
 836            loss_fn.last_time_diff2_loss = accumulated_time_diff2_loss / len(dataloader.dataset)
 837
 838        # Skip validation if num_valid is 0, use training loss instead
 839        if num_valid > 0:
 840            dataloader.dataset.set_initial_condition("random")
 841            dataloader.dataset.set_num_examples(num_valid)
 842
 843            # eval mode
 844            model.eval()
 845
 846            # prepare loss buffer for validation loss
 847            valid_loss = torch.zeros(2, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
 848
 849            # prepare metrics buffer for accumulation of validation metrics
 850            valid_metrics = {}
 851            for metric in metrics_fns:
 852                valid_metrics[metric] = torch.zeros(2, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
 853
 854            # perform validation
 855            with torch.no_grad():
 856                for inp, tar in dataloader:
 857                    # Check if tar is a trajectory (5D: B, T, C, H, W) or single target (4D: B, C, H, W)
 858                    if tar.dim() == 5:
 859                        # Multi-step trajectory: accumulate loss at each step
 860                        loss = 0
 861                        prd = inp
 862                        num_steps = tar.shape[1]
 863                        for step in range(num_steps):
 864                            prd = model(prd)
 865                            step_target = tar[:, step]  # (B, C, H, W)
 866                            loss = loss + loss_fn(prd, step_target, step=step)
 867                        # Average over steps
 868                        loss = (loss / num_steps).item()
 869                        # For metrics, use final prediction vs final target
 870                        final_target = tar[:, -1]
 871                    else:
 872                        # Single-step target: original behavior
 873                        prd = model(inp)
 874                        for _ in range(nfuture):
 875                            prd = model(prd)
 876                        loss = loss_fn(prd, tar, step=nfuture).item()
 877                        final_target = tar
 878
 879                    valid_loss[0] += loss * inp.size(0)
 880                    valid_loss[1] += inp.size(0)
 881
 882                    for metric in metrics_fns:
 883                        metric_buff = valid_metrics[metric]
 884                        metric_fn = metrics_fns[metric]
 885                        metric_buff[0] += metric_fn(prd, final_target) * inp.size(0)
 886                        metric_buff[1] += inp.size(0)
 887
 888            valid_loss = (valid_loss[0] / valid_loss[1]).item()
 889            for metric in valid_metrics:
 890                valid_metrics[metric] = (valid_metrics[metric][0] / valid_metrics[metric][1]).item()
 891        else:
 892            # No validation - use training loss for scheduler and checkpointing
 893            valid_loss = accumulated_loss
 894            valid_metrics = {}
 895
 896        if scheduler is not None:
 897            scheduler.step(valid_loss)
 898
 899        epoch_time = time.time() - epoch_start
 900
 901        if logging:
 902            print(f"--------------------------------------------------------------------------------")
 903            print(f"Epoch {epoch} summary:")
 904            print(f"time taken: {epoch_time:.2f}")
 905            print(f"accumulated training loss: {accumulated_loss}")
 906            
 907            # Detailed Loss Breakdown Table
 908            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_channel_losses'):
 909                print("\n  Loss Breakdown:")
 910                print(f"  {'Component':<20} | {'Raw Value':<12} | {'Lambda':<10} | {'Weighted':<12} | {'% Total':<8}")
 911                print("  " + "-"*75)
 912                
 913                loss_components = [
 914                    ("L2", "last_l2_loss", "l2_lambda"),
 915                    ("Laplacian", "last_lap_loss", "lap_lambda"),
 916                    ("Conservation", "last_cons_loss", "cons_lambda"),
 917                    ("CCC", "last_ccc_loss", "ccc_lambda"),
 918                    ("Spectral", "last_spectral_loss", "spectral_lambda"),
 919                    ("Gradient", "last_gradient_loss", "gradient_lambda"),
 920                    ("UV", "last_uv_loss", "uv_lambda"),
 921                    ("Spectral PV", "last_spectral_pv_loss", "spectral_pv_lambda"),
 922                    ("Spectral Mass", "last_mass_loss", "mass_lambda"),
 923                    ("Time Diff2", "last_time_diff2_loss", "time_diff2_lambda"),
 924                ]
 925                
 926                total_check = 0.0
 927                component_data = []
 928
 929                # First pass: collect data and total
 930                for name, attr_loss, attr_lambda in loss_components:
 931                    if hasattr(loss_fn, attr_loss) and hasattr(loss_fn, attr_lambda):
 932                        raw_val = getattr(loss_fn, attr_loss)
 933                        lam_val = getattr(loss_fn, attr_lambda)
 934                        if lam_val > 0:
 935                            weighted_val = raw_val * lam_val
 936                            component_data.append((name, raw_val, lam_val, weighted_val))
 937                            total_check += weighted_val
 938                
 939                # Second pass: print rows
 940                for name, raw_val, lam_val, weighted_val in component_data:
 941                    pct = (weighted_val / total_check * 100) if total_check > 0 else 0
 942                    print(f"  {name:<20} | {raw_val:<12.6f} | {lam_val:<10.2e} | {weighted_val:<12.6f} | {pct:<6.1f}%")
 943                print("  " + "-"*75 + "\n")
 944
 945            # Print per-variable losses
 946            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_channel_losses'):
 947                ch_losses = loss_fn.last_channel_losses
 948                print(f"  per-variable L2: height={ch_losses['height']:.6f}, vorticity={ch_losses['vorticity']:.6f}, divergence={ch_losses['divergence']:.6f}")
 949            if num_valid > 0:
 950                print(f"validation loss: {valid_loss}")
 951                for metric in valid_metrics:
 952                    print(f"{metric}: {valid_metrics[metric]}")
 953
 954        # Check and save best checkpoint AFTER printing epoch summary
 955        if valid_loss < best_valid_loss:
 956            best_valid_loss = valid_loss
 957            if hasattr(model, 'best_checkpoint_path'):
 958                if logging:
 959                    print(f"Training loss improved to {best_valid_loss:.6f}. Saving best checkpoint...")
 960                torch.save(model.state_dict(), model.best_checkpoint_path)
 961        
 962        # Always save recent checkpoint (every epoch)
 963        if hasattr(model, 'recent_checkpoint_path'):
 964            torch.save(model.state_dict(), model.recent_checkpoint_path)
 965
 966        # Save a copy to scratch directory if provided
 967        if scratch_dir is not None:
 968            if not os.path.exists(scratch_dir):
 969                os.makedirs(scratch_dir, exist_ok=True)
 970            scratch_checkpoint_path = os.path.join(scratch_dir, f"checkpoint_epoch_{epoch}.pt")
 971            torch.save(model.state_dict(), scratch_checkpoint_path)
 972
 973
 974        # Periodic plot logging (commit=False, so plots merge into the metrics log below)
 975        if plot_callback is not None:
 976            plot_callback(model, epoch_label=f"pretrain_ep{epoch}")
 977
 978        if logging:
 979            if wandb.run is not None:
 980                current_lr = optimizer.param_groups[0]["lr"]
 981                log_dict = {"loss": accumulated_loss, "learning rate": current_lr}
 982                # Log separate loss components if available
 983                if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_l2_loss'):
 984                    log_dict["L2 loss"] = loss_fn.last_l2_loss
 985                if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_lap_loss'):
 986                    log_dict["Laplacian loss"] = loss_fn.last_lap_loss
 987                if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_cons_loss'):
 988                    log_dict["Conservation loss"] = loss_fn.last_cons_loss
 989                if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_ccc_loss'):
 990                    log_dict["CCC loss"] = loss_fn.last_ccc_loss
 991                if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_spectral_loss'):
 992                    log_dict["Spectral loss"] = loss_fn.last_spectral_loss
 993                if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_gradient_loss'):
 994                    log_dict["Gradient loss"] = loss_fn.last_gradient_loss
 995                if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_uv_loss'):
 996                    log_dict["UV loss"] = loss_fn.last_uv_loss
 997                if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_spectral_pv_loss'):
 998                    log_dict["Spectral PV loss"] = loss_fn.last_spectral_pv_loss
 999                if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_mass_loss'):
1000                    log_dict["Spectral Mass loss"] = loss_fn.last_mass_loss
1001                if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_time_diff2_loss'):
1002                    log_dict["Time Diff2 loss"] = loss_fn.last_time_diff2_loss
1003                if num_valid > 0:
1004                    log_dict["validation loss"] = valid_loss
1005                    for metric in valid_metrics:
1006                        log_dict[metric] = valid_metrics[metric]
1007                wandb.log(log_dict)
1008
1009    train_time = time.time() - train_start
1010
1011    print(f"--------------------------------------------------------------------------------")
1012    print(f"done. Training took {train_time}.")
1013    return valid_loss
1014
1015# Curriculum rollout training: train on final step after variable-length gradient-free rollouts
1016def train_curriculum_rollout(
1017    model,
1018    dataset,
1019    loss_fn,
1020    optimizer,
1021    gscaler,
1022    scheduler=None,
1023    nepochs=50,
1024    num_examples=256,
1025    batch_size=4,
1026    amp_mode="none",
1027    max_steps_schedule=None,  # List: max_steps for each epoch, e.g., [1, 2, 4, 8, 16]
1028    logging=True,
1029    device=torch.device("cpu"),
1030    n_history=1,  # Number of past timesteps model expects (1=single-step, 3=AB3-like)
1031    noise_injection_sigma=0.0,
1032    noise_injection_rate=0.0,
1033    plot_callback=None,
1034    scratch_dir=None,
1035):
1036    """
1037    Curriculum rollout training mode with multi-history support.
1038    
1039    For each batch:
1040    1. Generate random IC -> normalize
1041    2. Run ML model for n_steps WITHOUT gradients (detached rollout)
1042       - For n_history>1: maintain sliding window history buffer
1043    3. Convert final ML state to spectral -> run solver 1 step -> normalize (ground truth)
1044    4. Run ML model 1 step WITH gradients (prediction)
1045    5. Compute loss -> backprop
1046    
1047    n_steps is randomly sampled from [0, current_max_steps] each batch.
1048    current_max_steps follows the schedule, increasing each epoch.
1049    """
1050    train_start = time.time()
1051    
1052    # Default schedule: linear ramp from 1 to 32 over nepochs
1053    if max_steps_schedule is None:
1054        max_steps_schedule = [min(1 + epoch, 32) for epoch in range(nepochs)]
1055    
1056    # Extend schedule if shorter than nepochs
1057    while len(max_steps_schedule) < nepochs:
1058        max_steps_schedule.append(max_steps_schedule[-1])
1059    
1060    # Set AMP type
1061    amp_dtype = torch.float32
1062    if amp_mode == "fp16":
1063        amp_dtype = torch.float16
1064    elif amp_mode == "bf16":
1065        amp_dtype = torch.bfloat16
1066    
1067    solver = dataset.solver
1068    inp_mean = dataset.inp_mean
1069    inp_var = dataset.inp_var
1070    nsteps = dataset.nsteps  # Solver steps per ML step
1071    
1072    best_loss = float('inf')
1073    
1074    for epoch in range(nepochs):
1075        epoch_start = time.time()
1076        current_max_steps = max_steps_schedule[epoch]
1077        
1078        model.train()
1079        accumulated_loss = 0.0
1080        accumulated_l2_loss = 0.0
1081        accumulated_lap_loss = 0.0
1082        accumulated_cons_loss = 0.0
1083        accumulated_ccc_loss = 0.0
1084        accumulated_spectral_loss = 0.0
1085        accumulated_gradient_loss = 0.0
1086        accumulated_spectral_pv_loss = 0.0
1087        accumulated_mass_loss = 0.0
1088        num_batches = (num_examples + batch_size - 1) // batch_size
1089        
1090        for batch_idx in range(num_batches):
1091            actual_batch_size = min(batch_size, num_examples - batch_idx * batch_size)
1092            
1093            # Randomly sample rollout length for this batch
1094            n_steps = torch.randint(0, current_max_steps + 1, (1,)).item()
1095            
1096            # Generate batch of random ICs and convert to normalized grid space
1097            batch_states = []
1098            batch_specs = []
1099            for _ in range(actual_batch_size):
1100                ic_spec = solver.random_initial_condition(mach=0.2)
1101                batch_specs.append(ic_spec)
1102                state = solver.spec2grid(ic_spec)
1103                state = (state - inp_mean) / torch.sqrt(inp_var)
1104                batch_states.append(state)
1105            
1106            # Stack into batch: (B, C, H, W)
1107            states = torch.stack(batch_states, dim=0)  # (B, C, H, W) - single timestep state
1108            
1109            # --- Noise Injection Logic (Curriculum Rollout) ---
1110            # For curriculum training, we inject noise at the very beginning (t=0)
1111            if noise_injection_sigma > 0 and noise_injection_rate > 0 and states.size(0) >= 2:
1112                batch_size = states.size(0)
1113                half_batch = batch_size // 2
1114                
1115                for i in range(half_batch):
1116                    if torch.rand(1).item() < noise_injection_rate:
1117                        target_idx = i + half_batch
1118                        
1119                        # Duplicate initial state
1120                        states[target_idx] = states[i].clone()
1121                        
1122                        # Add noise
1123                        noise = torch.randn_like(states[target_idx]) * noise_injection_sigma
1124                        states[target_idx] += noise
1125                        
1126                        # Note: The ground truth generation (Phase 2) will start from this *noisy* state.
1127                        # The solver will filter it immediately, so the generated ground truth 
1128                        # will be clean (filtered) and correct. This is exactly what we want.
1129            # --------------------------------------------------
1130            
1131            # Initialize history buffer for multi-step models
1132            # Each sample has n_history states, buffer shape: list of (B, C, H, W)
1133            if n_history > 1:
1134                history_buffer = [states.clone() for _ in range(n_history)]
1135            
1136            # Phase 1: Gradient-free rollout for n_steps (batched)
1137            with torch.no_grad():
1138                for _ in range(n_steps):
1139                    if n_history > 1:
1140                        # Stack history: (B, n_history*C, H, W)
1141                        model_input = torch.cat(history_buffer, dim=1)
1142                    else:
1143                        model_input = states
1144                    
1145                    states = model(model_input)  # Output: (B, C, H, W)
1146                    
1147                    # Update history buffer (sliding window)
1148                    if n_history > 1:
1149                        history_buffer = history_buffer[1:] + [states.clone()]
1150            
1151            # Phase 2: Generate ground truth for each sample
1152            # (solver operates per-sample, so we still loop here)
1153            gt_list = []
1154            for i in range(actual_batch_size):
1155                # Un-normalize to get physical values
1156                state_physical = states[i] * torch.sqrt(inp_var.squeeze(0)) + inp_mean.squeeze(0)
1157                
1158                # Convert to spectral for solver
1159                state_spec = solver.grid2spec(state_physical)
1160                
1161                # Run solver one step to get ground truth
1162                gt_spec = solver.timestep(state_spec, nsteps)
1163                gt_grid = solver.spec2grid(gt_spec)
1164                gt_normalized = (gt_grid - inp_mean.squeeze(0)) / torch.sqrt(inp_var.squeeze(0))
1165                gt_list.append(gt_normalized)
1166            
1167            # Stack ground truth: (B, C, H, W)
1168            gt_batch = torch.stack(gt_list, dim=0)
1169            
1170            # Phase 3: One ML step WITH gradients (batched)
1171            with torch.autocast(device_type="cuda", dtype=amp_dtype, enabled=(amp_mode != "none")):
1172                # Identify prev_state and prev_prev_state
1173                prev_state = None
1174                prev_prev_state = None
1175                
1176                if n_history > 1:
1177                    prev_state = history_buffer[-1]
1178                    prev_prev_state = history_buffer[-2]
1179                else:
1180                    prev_state = states # This is the state used to generate pred
1181                    prev_prev_state = None # History not tracked in single step curriculum loop easily?
1182                    # Actually we can track it if we wanted, but let's stick to what's available
1183                
1184                # In curriculum/one-step-ahead mode, GT history is identical to Model history
1185                prev_target = prev_state
1186                prev_prev_target = prev_prev_state
1187
1188                pred = model(model_input)
1189                batch_loss = loss_fn(pred, gt_batch, 
1190                                     prev_state=prev_state, 
1191                                     prev_prev_state=prev_prev_state,
1192                                     prev_target=prev_target,
1193                                     prev_prev_target=prev_prev_target)
1194            
1195            accumulated_loss += batch_loss.item() * actual_batch_size
1196            # Accumulate loss components if available
1197            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_l2_loss'):
1198                accumulated_l2_loss += loss_fn.last_l2_loss * actual_batch_size
1199            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_lap_loss'):
1200                accumulated_lap_loss += loss_fn.last_lap_loss * actual_batch_size
1201            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_cons_loss'):
1202                accumulated_cons_loss += loss_fn.last_cons_loss * actual_batch_size
1203            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_ccc_loss'):
1204                accumulated_ccc_loss += loss_fn.last_ccc_loss * actual_batch_size
1205            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_spectral_loss'):
1206                accumulated_spectral_loss += loss_fn.last_spectral_loss * actual_batch_size
1207            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_gradient_loss'):
1208                accumulated_gradient_loss += loss_fn.last_gradient_loss * actual_batch_size
1209            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_spectral_pv_loss'):
1210                accumulated_spectral_pv_loss += loss_fn.last_spectral_pv_loss * actual_batch_size
1211            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_mass_loss'):
1212                accumulated_mass_loss += loss_fn.last_mass_loss * actual_batch_size
1213            
1214            # Backprop
1215            optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
1216            gscaler.scale(batch_loss).backward()
1217            gscaler.step(optimizer)
1218            gscaler.update()
1219        
1220        accumulated_loss = accumulated_loss / num_examples
1221        # Average loss components and store for logging
1222        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_l2_loss'):
1223            loss_fn.last_l2_loss = accumulated_l2_loss / num_examples
1224        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_lap_loss'):
1225            loss_fn.last_lap_loss = accumulated_lap_loss / num_examples
1226        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_cons_loss'):
1227            loss_fn.last_cons_loss = accumulated_cons_loss / num_examples
1228        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_ccc_loss'):
1229            loss_fn.last_ccc_loss = accumulated_ccc_loss / num_examples
1230        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_spectral_loss'):
1231            loss_fn.last_spectral_loss = accumulated_spectral_loss / num_examples
1232        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_gradient_loss'):
1233            loss_fn.last_gradient_loss = accumulated_gradient_loss / num_examples
1234        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_spectral_pv_loss'):
1235            loss_fn.last_spectral_pv_loss = accumulated_spectral_pv_loss / num_examples
1236        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_mass_loss'):
1237            loss_fn.last_mass_loss = accumulated_mass_loss / num_examples
1238        
1239        if scheduler is not None:
1240            scheduler.step(accumulated_loss)
1241        
1242        epoch_time = time.time() - epoch_start
1243        
1244        if logging:
1245            print(f"--------------------------------------------------------------------------------")
1246            print(f"Curriculum Epoch {epoch} (max_steps={current_max_steps}):")
1247            print(f"  time: {epoch_time:.2f}s, loss: {accumulated_loss:.6f}")
1248            
1249            # Detailed Loss Breakdown Table (Curriculum)
1250            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_channel_losses'):
1251                print("\n  Loss Breakdown:")
1252                print(f"  {'Component':<20} | {'Raw Value':<12} | {'Lambda':<10} | {'Weighted':<12} | {'% Total':<8}")
1253                print("  " + "-"*75)
1254                
1255                loss_components = [
1256                    ("L2", "last_l2_loss", "l2_lambda"),
1257                    ("Laplacian", "last_lap_loss", "lap_lambda"),
1258                    ("Conservation", "last_cons_loss", "cons_lambda"),
1259                    ("CCC", "last_ccc_loss", "ccc_lambda"),
1260                    ("Spectral", "last_spectral_loss", "spectral_lambda"),
1261                    ("Gradient", "last_gradient_loss", "gradient_lambda"),
1262                    ("UV", "last_uv_loss", "uv_lambda"),
1263                    ("Spectral PV", "last_spectral_pv_loss", "spectral_pv_lambda"),
1264                    ("Spectral Mass", "last_mass_loss", "mass_lambda"),
1265                ]
1266                
1267                total_check = 0.0
1268                component_data = []
1269
1270                # First pass: collect data and total
1271                for name, attr_loss, attr_lambda in loss_components:
1272                    if hasattr(loss_fn, attr_loss) and hasattr(loss_fn, attr_lambda):
1273                        raw_val = getattr(loss_fn, attr_loss)
1274                        lam_val = getattr(loss_fn, attr_lambda)
1275                        if lam_val > 0:
1276                            weighted_val = raw_val * lam_val
1277                            component_data.append((name, raw_val, lam_val, weighted_val))
1278                            total_check += weighted_val
1279                
1280                # Second pass: print rows
1281                for name, raw_val, lam_val, weighted_val in component_data:
1282                    pct = (weighted_val / total_check * 100) if total_check > 0 else 0
1283                    print(f"  {name:<20} | {raw_val:<12.6f} | {lam_val:<10.2e} | {weighted_val:<12.6f} | {pct:<6.1f}%")
1284                print("  " + "-"*75 + "\n")
1285        
1286        # Save best checkpoint
1287        if accumulated_loss < best_loss:
1288            best_loss = accumulated_loss
1289            if hasattr(model, 'best_checkpoint_path'):
1290                if logging:
1291                    print(f"  New best loss! Saving checkpoint...")
1292                torch.save(model.state_dict(), model.best_checkpoint_path)
1293        
1294        # Always save recent checkpoint (every epoch)
1295        if hasattr(model, 'recent_checkpoint_path'):
1296            torch.save(model.state_dict(), model.recent_checkpoint_path)
1297            
1298        # Save a copy to scratch directory if provided
1299        if scratch_dir is not None:
1300            if not os.path.exists(scratch_dir):
1301                os.makedirs(scratch_dir, exist_ok=True)
1302            scratch_checkpoint_path = os.path.join(scratch_dir, f"curriculum_checkpoint_{epoch}.pt")
1303            torch.save(model.state_dict(), scratch_checkpoint_path)
1304
1305        
1306        # Periodic plot logging (commit=False, so plots merge into the metrics log below)
1307        if plot_callback is not None:
1308            plot_callback(model, epoch_label=f"curriculum_ep{epoch}")
1309
1310        # Log to wandb
1311        if logging and wandb is not None and wandb.run is not None:
1312            current_lr = optimizer.param_groups[0]["lr"]
1313            log_dict = {
1314                "curriculum_loss": accumulated_loss,
1315                "curriculum_max_steps": current_max_steps,
1316                "curriculum_lr": current_lr,
1317            }
1318            # Log separate loss components if available
1319            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_l2_loss'):
1320                log_dict["L2 loss"] = loss_fn.last_l2_loss
1321            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_lap_loss'):
1322                log_dict["Laplacian loss"] = loss_fn.last_lap_loss
1323            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_cons_loss'):
1324                log_dict["Conservation loss"] = loss_fn.last_cons_loss
1325            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_ccc_loss'):
1326                log_dict["CCC loss"] = loss_fn.last_ccc_loss
1327            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_spectral_loss'):
1328                log_dict["Spectral loss"] = loss_fn.last_spectral_loss
1329            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_gradient_loss'):
1330                log_dict["Gradient loss"] = loss_fn.last_gradient_loss
1331            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_uv_loss'):
1332                log_dict["UV loss"] = loss_fn.last_uv_loss
1333            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_spectral_pv_loss'):
1334                log_dict["Spectral PV loss"] = loss_fn.last_spectral_pv_loss
1335            if hasattr(loss_fn, 'last_mass_loss'):
1336                log_dict["Spectral Mass loss"] = loss_fn.last_mass_loss
1337            wandb.log(log_dict)
1338    
1339    train_time = time.time() - train_start
1340    print(f"--------------------------------------------------------------------------------")
1341    print(f"Curriculum training done. Took {train_time:.2f}s")
1342    return accumulated_loss
1343
1344
1345def parse_list_arg(value, dtype=int):
1346    """Parse a comma-separated string or single value into a list.
1347    
1348    Args:
1349        value: Either a single value, or a comma-separated string (e.g., "12,6,6")
1350        dtype: Type to convert each element to (int or float)
1351    
1352    Returns:
1353        List of values of the specified type
1354    """
1355    if isinstance(value, str):
1356        return [dtype(x.strip()) for x in value.split(",")]
1357    else:
1358        return [dtype(value)]
1359
1360
1361def main(root_path, pretrain_epochs=100, finetune_epochs=10, batch_size=1, learning_rate=1e-3, train=True, load_checkpoint=False, checkpoint_name="checkpoint.pt", amp_mode="none", log_grads=0, validate_steps=96, nics=50, finetune_steps=2, curriculum_epochs=0, curriculum_max_steps=None, seed=None, l2_lambda=1.0, laplacian_lambda=0.0, conservation_lambda=0.0, ccc_lambda=0.0, spectral_lambda=0.0, gradient_lambda=0.0, vorticity_lambda=1.0, uv_lambda=0.0, spectral_pv_lambda=0.0, mass_lambda=0.0, time_diff2_lambda=0.0, run_tag=None, num_examples=256, ic_mach=0.2, ic_llimit=25, stochastic_ic_llimit=False, stochastic_ic_mach=False, plotting_dt=0, ic_spinup_max=0, n_history=1, precomputed_db_path=None, first_step_lambda=1.0, first_step_only_l2_lap_uv=False, checkpoint_activation=False):
1362
1363    # enable logging by default
1364    logging = True
1365
1366    # set seed (random if not specified)
1367    if seed is None:
1368        seed = torch.randint(0, 2**31, (1,)).item()
1369    print(f"Using random seed: {seed}")
1370    torch.manual_seed(seed)
1371    torch.cuda.manual_seed(seed)
1372
1373    # set device
1374    device = torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
1375    if torch.cuda.is_available():
1376        torch.cuda.set_device(device.index)
1377
1378    # 30 minute prediction steps. note: the attention models have "stability
1379    # issues" for dt=3600s, so we lower to 1800s. (solution falls apart using
1380    # autoregressive rollout) LSNO and SFNO are stable at 3600s.
1381    dt = 600# 1800 #1 * 1800
1382    dt_solver = 150
1383    nsteps = dt // dt_solver
1384    grid = "equiangular"
1385    nlat, nlon = (192, 288)
1386    # dt = 3600 #1 * 1800
1387    # dt_solver = 150
1388    # nsteps = dt // dt_solver
1389    # grid = "equiangular"
1390    # nlat, nlon = (256, 512)
1391    dataset = PdeDatasetExtended(
1392        dt=dt, nsteps=nsteps, dims=(nlat, nlon), device=device, grid=grid, normalize=True, nfuture=0,
1393        ic_mach=ic_mach,
1394        ic_llimit=ic_llimit,
1395        stochastic_ic_llimit=stochastic_ic_llimit,
1396        stochastic_ic_mach=stochastic_ic_mach,
1397        ic_spinup_max=ic_spinup_max,
1398        num_examples=num_examples
1399    )
1400    dataset.sht = RealSHT(nlat=nlat, nlon=nlon, grid=grid).to(device=device)
1401    
1402    # Override single-sample normalization with robust equilibrium stats
1403    mach = ic_mach  # Match the mach number used in PdeDataset._get_sample()
1404    norm_stats = get_or_compute_stats(
1405        solver=dataset.solver,
1406        cache_dir=root_path,
1407        n_samples=1000,      # Number of equilibrated samples for statistics
1408        mach=mach,
1409        spinup_steps=50,    # Run solver 100 steps to reach equilibrium
1410        dt=dt,
1411        dt_solver=dt_solver,
1412        show_progress=True
1413    )
1414    dataset.inp_mean = norm_stats["mean"]
1415    dataset.inp_var = norm_stats["var"]
1416    
1417    # Compute global U and V standard deviations separately for loss normalization
1418    # uv_var is (2, 1, 1), indices 0=u, 1=v
1419    if "uv_var" in norm_stats:
1420        uv_var = norm_stats["uv_var"]
1421        u_std = torch.sqrt(uv_var[0]).mean().item()
1422        v_std = torch.sqrt(uv_var[1]).mean().item()
1423    else:
1424        # Fallback for old stats files
1425        print("Warning: 'uv_var' key missing in stats file. Falling back to global var.")
1426        u_std = torch.sqrt(dataset.inp_var.mean()).item() 
1427        v_std = torch.sqrt(dataset.inp_var.mean()).item()
1428        
1429    print(f"Computed UV normalization stds: U={u_std:.2f}, V={v_std:.2f} m/s")
1430    
1431    precomputed_ds = None
1432    # Conditional training DataLoader: precomputed Zarr DB or on-the-fly solver
1433    if args.precomputed_db:
1434        from precomputed_dataset import PrecomputedTrajectoryDataset
1435        zarr_path = os.path.join(args.precomputed_db, "trajectories.zarr")
1436        stats_path = os.path.join(args.precomputed_db, "stats.npz")
1437        precomputed_ds = PrecomputedTrajectoryDataset(
1438            zarr_path=zarr_path,
1439            stats_path=stats_path,
1440            n_history=n_history,
1441            nfuture=0,
1442            normalize=True,
1443        )
1444        # Zarr is process-safe, so we can use multi-worker loading
1445        # Lowering to 4 workers to avoid saturating 8 CPU allocation
1446        # Use RandomSampler to limit epoch size to num_examples (not whole dataset).
1447        #
1448        # IMPORTANT: We must rebuild the DataLoader (not just mutate dataset.nfuture)
1449        # because persistent_workers=True forks workers once on first iteration and
1450        # each worker holds its own frozen copy of the dataset object. Setting
1451        # dataset.nfuture on the main process never reaches the workers, so they
1452        # would keep serving single-step targets even during autoregressive finetuning.
1453        def _make_precomputed_loader(nfuture_val):
1454            """Rebuild the DataLoader so workers see the updated nfuture window."""
1455            precomputed_ds.nfuture = nfuture_val  # rebuilds self.index in-place
1456            sampler = RandomSampler(precomputed_ds, replacement=True,
1457                                    num_samples=num_examples)
1458            return DataLoader(precomputed_ds, batch_size=batch_size,
1459                              sampler=sampler, num_workers=14,
1460                              prefetch_factor=16, persistent_workers=True,
1461                              pin_memory=True)
1462
1463        dataloader = _make_precomputed_loader(nfuture_val=0)
1464        print(f"Using pre-computed training data from: {args.precomputed_db}")
1465        print(f"  {len(precomputed_ds)} total samples. Sampling {num_examples} per epoch.")
1466        print("  Starting training loop...")
1467
1468        # Override u_std/v_std from the DB's own stats.npz so the UV loss is
1469        # normalized against the actual distribution of the training data.
1470        db_stats = np.load(stats_path)
1471        if "uv_var" in db_stats and not np.allclose(db_stats["uv_var"], 0.0):
1472            uv_var_db = torch.from_numpy(db_stats["uv_var"])
1473            u_std = torch.sqrt(uv_var_db[0]).mean().item()
1474            v_std = torch.sqrt(uv_var_db[1]).mean().item()
1475            print(f"  UV stds from DB stats.npz: U={u_std:.2f}, V={v_std:.2f} m/s")
1476        else:
1477            print("  WARNING: uv_var missing or zero in stats.npz — re-run generate_training_db.py "
1478                  "without --skip_stats, or run compute_db_stats.py to add UV stats.")
1479    else:
1480        # On-the-fly solver generation (original path)
1481        # There is still an issue with parallel dataloading. Do NOT use it at the moment
1482        dataloader = DataLoader(dataset, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=True, num_workers=0, persistent_workers=False)
1483
1484
1485    nlat = dataset.nlat
1486    nlon = dataset.nlon
1487
1488    # prepare dicts containing models and corresponding metrics
1489    models = {}
1490
1491    # Calculate input channels based on n_history
1492    # 3 channels per timestep (height, vorticity, divergence)
1493    in_chans = 3 * n_history
1494
1495    # get baseline model registry
1496    baseline_models = get_baseline_models(img_size=(nlat, nlon), in_chans=in_chans, out_chans=3, residual_prediction=True, grid=grid, checkpoint_activation=checkpoint_activation)
1497
1498    # specify which models to train here
1499    # Format: "Run_Name": baseline_models["Architecture_Name"]
1500    models = {
1501        #  "sfno_sc2_layers4_e32": baseline_models["sfno_sc2_layers4_e32"],
1502        #  "sfno_sc2_layers4_e128_FR": baseline_models["sfno_sc2_layers4_e128"],
1503        #  "nnorm_gam29_d600": baseline_models["nnorm_gam2"],
1504        #  "disco_mlp_transformer_e64": baseline_models["disco_mlp_transformer_e64"],
1505        #  "EPD_W9_MB4_D32": baseline_models["encode_process_decode"],
1506        #  "EPD_W9_MB1_D66_L": baseline_models["EPD_W9_MB1_D66_L"],
1507        #  "disco_epd_W9_MB1_D66_L": baseline_models["disco_epd_W9_MB1_D66_L"],
1508        # "disco_epd_W9_MB1_D66_L_morlet": baseline_models["disco_epd_W9_MB1_D66_L_morlet"],
1509        #  "hierarchical_test_5x5": baseline_models["hierarchical_disco_epd"]
1510        # "sfno_sc2_layers4_e32_T1": baseline_models["sfno_sc2_layers4_e32"],
1511        # "film_filtered_diffusion_AR6_2": baseline_models["film_filtered_diffusion"],
1512        # "film_curriculum_rollout_T2": baseline_models["film"],
1513        # "film_filtered_diffusion_curriculum_ro
1514        # llout_T2": baseline_models["film_filtered_diffusion"],
1515        # "film_filtered_diffusion_curriculum_reg_sched": baseline_models["film_filtered_diffusion"],
1516        # "film_laplacian_8": baseline_models["film"],
1517        # "film_laplacian_8": baseline_models["film_conservation"],
1518        # "film_L7_MC5_H3": baseline_models["film_history3"],
1519        # "film_history3_conservation_CCC7_l23": baseline_models["film_history3_conservation"],
1520        # "film_conservation_CCC0_l21": baseline_models["film_conservation"],
1521        # "noise_T1_stochastic_lic_half": baseline_models["film_conservation"],
1522        # "noise_T1_stochastic_lic_half2": baseline_models["film_conservation"],
1523        # "plot_testing_icsu100_noise_laplacian": baseline_models["film_conservation"],
1524        # "film_conservation_precomputed_ctrl": baseline_models["film_conservation"],
1525        # "film_conservation_spectral_norm": baseline_models["film_conservation_spectral_norm"],
1526        # "s2ntransformer_lap8_ccc1_l23": baseline_models["s2ntransformer_sc2_layers4_e128"],
1527        # "noise_laplacian_history3": baseline_models["film_conservation_history3"],
1528        # "mlp_transformer_e64_h2_w9_spectral": baseline_models["mlp_transformer_e64_h2"],
1529        # "mlp_transformer_e126_h4_control": baseline_models["mlp_transformer_e126_h4"],
1530        # "disco_mlp_transformer_e64_control": baseline_models["disco_mlp_transformer_e64"],
1531        # "mlp_transformer_e126_h4_no_lat_control": baseline_models["mlp_transformer_e126_h4_no_lat"],
1532        # "s2ntransformer_sc2_layers4_e128_control2": baseline_models["s2ntransformer_sc2_layers4_e128"],
1533        # "s2ntransformer_sc2_layers4_e128_k55_tc9": baseline_models["s2ntransformer_sc2_layers4_e128_k55_tc9"],
1534        # "s2unet_sc2_layers4_e128_morlet_L2_only": baseline_models["s2unet_sc2_layers4_e128"],
1535        # "s2ntransformer_precomputed_ctrl": baseline_models["s2ntransformer_sc2_layers4_e128"],
1536        # "s2ntransformer_sc2_layers4_e128_L2_NS8192_Lap8Noise": baseline_models["s2ntransformer_sc2_layers4_e128"],
1537         "sfno_sc2_layers4_e128_L2_NS8192_FSL_T1": baseline_models["sfno_sc2_layers4_e128"],
1538        #  "sfno_sc3_layers4_e128_L2": baseline_models["sfno_sc3_layers4_e128"],
1539        #  "lsno_sc2_layers4_e128_NS8192_Lap8": baseline_models["lsno_sc2_layers4_e128"],
1540
1541
1542
1543
1544    }
1545
1546    # project_str = "cons_and_attn"
1547    # project_str = "time_laplacian"
1548    project_str = "precomputed_2"
1549    # project_str = "mlp_transformer_testing"
1550
1551    # Available Architectures in baseline_models:
1552    # "s2transformer_sc2_layers4_e128", # Spherical Transformer (Global Attention)
1553    # "s2ntransformer_sc2_layers4_e128",# Spherical Transformer (Neighborhood Attention)
1554    # "s2segformer_sc2_layers4_e128",   # Spherical Segformer (Global Attention)
1555    # "s2nsegformer_sc2_layers4_e128",  # Spherical Segformer (Neighborhood Attention)
1556    # "sfno_sc2_layers4_e32",           # SFNO
1557    # "lsno_sc2_layers4_e32",           # LSNO
1558
1559    # loss function
1560    l2_loss_fn = SquaredL2LossS2(nlat=nlat, nlon=nlon, grid=grid).to(device)
1561    
1562    # optional loss components
1563    lap_loss_fn = LaplacianLossS2(nlat=nlat, nlon=nlon, grid=grid).to(device) if laplacian_lambda > 0 else None
1564    cons_loss_fn = ConservationLossS2(nlat=nlat, nlon=nlon, grid=grid).to(device) if conservation_lambda > 0 else None
1565    spectral_loss_fn = SpectralLossS2(nlat=nlat, nlon=nlon, grid=grid, lmin_loss=args.spectral_lmin, lmax_loss=args.spectral_lmax, decay_exponent=args.spectral_decay).to(device) if args.spectral_lambda > 0 else None
1566    gradient_loss_fn = GradientLossS2(nlat=nlat, nlon=nlon, grid=grid).to(device) if args.gradient_lambda > 0 else None
1567    
1568    # Wrapper class to track all loss components
1569    class CombinedLoss(nn.Module):
1570        CHANNEL_NAMES = ["height", "vorticity", "divergence"]
1571        
1572        
1573        def __init__(self, l2_fn, lap_fn=None, cons_fn=None, ccc_fn=None, spectral_fn=None, gradient_fn=None,
1574                 l2_lambda=1.0, lap_lambda=0.0, cons_lambda=0.0, ccc_lambda=0.0, spectral_lambda=0.0, gradient_lambda=0.0, 
1575                 vorticity_lambda=1.0, uv_lambda=0.0, spectral_pv_lambda=0.0, mass_lambda=0.0, time_diff2_lambda=0.0,
1576                 first_step_lambda=1.0, first_step_only_l2_lap_uv=False,
1577                 solver=None, inp_mean=None, inp_var=None, u_std=None, v_std=None, device=None):
1578            super().__init__()
1579            self.l2_fn = l2_fn
1580            self.lap_fn = lap_fn
1581            self.cons_fn = cons_fn
1582            self.ccc_fn = ccc_fn
1583            self.spectral_fn = spectral_fn
1584            self.gradient_fn = gradient_fn
1585            self.l2_lambda = l2_lambda
1586            self.lap_lambda = lap_lambda
1587            self.cons_lambda = cons_lambda
1588            self.ccc_lambda = ccc_lambda
1589            self.spectral_lambda = spectral_lambda
1590            self.gradient_lambda = gradient_lambda
1591            self.vorticity_lambda = vorticity_lambda
1592            self.uv_lambda = uv_lambda
1593            self.spectral_pv_lambda = spectral_pv_lambda
1594            self.mass_lambda = mass_lambda
1595            self.time_diff2_lambda = time_diff2_lambda
1596            self.first_step_lambda = first_step_lambda
1597            self.first_step_only_l2_lap_uv = first_step_only_l2_lap_uv
1598            
1599            # UV Reconstruction Loss params and Spectral PV/Mass Loss params
1600            self.solver = solver
1601            self.inp_mean = inp_mean
1602            self.inp_var = inp_var
1603            self.u_std = u_std
1604            self.v_std = v_std
1605            
1606            # Initialize Spectral PV Loss if enabled
1607            self.pv_loss_fn = None
1608            if self.spectral_pv_lambda > 0 and self.solver is not None and TwoStepPVResidual is not None:
1609                 # Check for cached lats in solver
1610                if hasattr(self.solver, "sht") and hasattr(self.solver.sht, "lats"):
1611                     # solver.sht.lats are usually colatitudes in radians (0 to pi)
1612                     colat_rad = self.solver.sht.lats
1613                     lat_deg = 90.0 - torch.rad2deg(colat_rad)
1614                else:
1615                    lat_deg = np.linspace(-90, 90, self.solver.nlat)
1616                
1617                if device is None:
1618                    device = torch.device('cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu')
1619
1620                self.pv_loss_fn = TwoStepPVResidual(
1621                    lats_deg=lat_deg,
1622                    dt_seconds=600.0, # Fixed DT for this setup
1623                    omega=getattr(self.solver, 'omega', 7.292e-5),
1624                    radius=getattr(self.solver, 'radius', 6.37122e6),
1625                    gravity=getattr(self.solver, 'gravity', 9.80616),
