1626                    mode="pv_advective",
1627                    height_mode="geopotential" # Data is geopotential
1628                ).to(device=device)
1629
1630            # Initialize Spectral Mass Loss if enabled
1631            self.mass_loss_fn = None
1632            if self.mass_lambda > 0 and self.solver is not None and TwoStepMassResidual is not None:
1633                 # Check for cached lats in solver
1634                if hasattr(self.solver, "sht") and hasattr(self.solver.sht, "lats"):
1635                     # solver.sht.lats are usually colatitudes in radians (0 to pi)
1636                     colat_rad = self.solver.sht.lats
1637                     lat_deg = 90.0 - torch.rad2deg(colat_rad)
1638                else:
1639                    lat_deg = np.linspace(-90, 90, self.solver.nlat)
1640                
1641                if device is None:
1642                    device = torch.device('cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu')
1643
1644                self.mass_loss_fn = TwoStepMassResidual(
1645                    lats_deg=lat_deg,
1646                    dt_seconds=600.0, # Fixed DT for this setup
1647                    height_mode="geopotential" # Data is geopotential
1648                ).to(device=device)
1649            
1650            # Store accumulated values for logging
1651            self.last_l2_loss = 0.0
1652            self.last_lap_loss = 0.0
1653            self.last_cons_loss = 0.0
1654            self.last_ccc_loss = 0.0
1655            self.last_spectral_loss = 0.0
1656            self.last_gradient_loss = 0.0
1657            self.last_uv_loss = 0.0
1658            self.last_spectral_pv_loss = 0.0
1659            self.last_mass_loss = 0.0
1660            self.last_time_diff2_loss = 0.0
1661            self.last_channel_losses = {name: 0.0 for name in self.CHANNEL_NAMES}
1662            
1663        def reset_stats(self):
1664            """Reset the step accumulations at the beginning of a rollout."""
1665            self.last_l2_loss = 0.0
1666            self.last_lap_loss = 0.0
1667            self.last_cons_loss = 0.0
1668            self.last_ccc_loss = 0.0
1669            self.last_spectral_loss = 0.0
1670            self.last_gradient_loss = 0.0
1671            self.last_uv_loss = 0.0
1672            self.last_spectral_pv_loss = 0.0
1673            self.last_mass_loss = 0.0
1674            self.last_time_diff2_loss = 0.0
1675            for k in self.last_channel_losses:
1676                self.last_channel_losses[k] = 0.0
1677        
1678        def forward(self, pred, target, prev_state=None, prev_prev_state=None, prev_target=None, prev_prev_target=None, step=0):
1679            """Compute combined loss with optional vorticity weighting."""
1680            total = torch.tensor(0.0, device=pred.device, dtype=pred.dtype)
1681            
1682            # Determine effective lambdas for the current step
1683            eff_l2_lambda = self.l2_lambda
1684            eff_lap_lambda = self.lap_lambda
1685            eff_uv_lambda = self.uv_lambda
1686            
1687            mask = 1.0
1688            if self.first_step_only_l2_lap_uv and step > 0:
1689                eff_l2_lambda = 0.0
1690                eff_lap_lambda = 0.0
1691                eff_uv_lambda = 0.0
1692                mask = 0.0
1693                
1694            step_mult = self.first_step_lambda if step == 0 else 1.0
1695                
1696            # L2 Loss (with optional vorticity weighting)
1697            if eff_l2_lambda > 0:
1698                # Compute per-channel L2 losses
1699                channel_losses = []
1700                for c, name in enumerate(self.CHANNEL_NAMES):
1701                    if c < pred.shape[1]:  # Safety check
1702                        ch_loss = self.l2_fn(pred[:, c:c+1], target[:, c:c+1])
1703                        self.last_channel_losses[name] = ch_loss.item()
1704                        channel_losses.append(ch_loss)
1705                
1706                # Apply vorticity weighting (channel index 1)
1707                if self.vorticity_lambda != 1.0 and len(channel_losses) > 1:
1708                    # Simple weighted sum: height + vorticity_lambda*vorticity + divergence
1709                    l2 = channel_losses[0] + self.vorticity_lambda * channel_losses[1] + channel_losses[2]
1710                else:
1711                    # Standard L2 (all channels weighted equally)
1712                    l2 = self.l2_fn(pred, target)
1713                
1714                self.last_l2_loss += l2.item() * step_mult * mask
1715                total = total + eff_l2_lambda * l2
1716            
1717            # Laplacian Loss
1718            if self.lap_fn is not None and eff_lap_lambda > 0:
1719                lap = self.lap_fn(pred, target)
1720                self.last_lap_loss += lap.item() * step_mult * mask
1721                total = total + eff_lap_lambda * lap
1722            
1723            # Conservation Loss
1724            if self.cons_fn is not None and self.cons_lambda > 0:
1725                cons = self.cons_fn(pred, target)
1726                self.last_cons_loss += cons.item() * step_mult
1727                total = total + self.cons_lambda * cons
1728            
1729            # CCC Loss (needs channel-last format)
1730            if self.ccc_fn is not None and self.ccc_lambda > 0:
1731                # CCC expects (B, H, W, C), we have (B, C, H, W)
1732                pred_nhwc = pred.permute(0, 2, 3, 1)
1733                target_nhwc = target.permute(0, 2, 3, 1)
1734                ccc = self.ccc_fn(pred_nhwc, target_nhwc)
1735                self.last_ccc_loss += ccc.item() * step_mult
1736                total = total + self.ccc_lambda * ccc
1737                
1738            # Spectral Loss
1739            if self.spectral_fn is not None and self.spectral_lambda > 0:
1740                spec = self.spectral_fn(pred, target)
1741                self.last_spectral_loss += spec.item() * step_mult
1742                total = total + self.spectral_lambda * spec
1743                
1744            # Gradient Loss
1745            if self.gradient_fn is not None and self.gradient_lambda > 0:
1746                grad = self.gradient_fn(pred, target)
1747                self.last_gradient_loss += grad.item() * step_mult
1748                total = total + self.gradient_lambda * grad
1749                
1750            # UV Reconstruction Loss
1751            if eff_uv_lambda > 0 and self.solver is not None:
1752                # 1. Denormalize to get physical grid values
1753                # pred/target: (B, C, H, W)
1754                pred_phys = pred * torch.sqrt(self.inp_var) + self.inp_mean
1755                target_phys = target * torch.sqrt(self.inp_var) + self.inp_mean
1756                
1757                # 2. Convert to spectral space
1758                # grid2spec expects (C, H, W), so we iterate over batch
1759                # Typically batch_size is small (1) in this script
1760                uv_loss = 0.0
1761                bs = pred.shape[0]
1762                for i in range(bs):
1763                    pred_spec = self.solver.grid2spec(pred_phys[i])
1764                    target_spec = self.solver.grid2spec(target_phys[i])
1765                    
1766                    # 3. Compute U/V from vorticity/divergence (indices 1:)
1767                    # getuv returns (2, H, W) -> u, v
1768                    pred_uv = self.solver.getuv(pred_spec[1:])     # (2, H, W)
1769                    target_uv = self.solver.getuv(target_spec[1:]) # (2, H, W)
1770                    
1771                    # 4. Normalize separately by u_std and v_std
1772                    # u component (index 0)
1773                    diff_u = (pred_uv[0] - target_uv[0]) / self.u_std
1774                    # v component (index 1)
1775                    diff_v = (pred_uv[1] - target_uv[1]) / self.v_std
1776                    
1777                    # MSE (sum of squares of both components)
1778                    uv_loss += (diff_u ** 2).mean() + (diff_v ** 2).mean()
1779                
1780                # Average over batch
1781                uv_loss = uv_loss / bs
1782                
1783                self.last_uv_loss += uv_loss.item() * step_mult * mask
1784                total = total + eff_uv_lambda * uv_loss
1785            
1786            # Spectral PV Loss
1787            if self.spectral_pv_lambda > 0 and self.pv_loss_fn is not None and prev_state is not None:
1788                # 1. Prepare states
1789                # prev_state might be stacked (B, n*C, H, W). We need the last state (B, C, H, W).
1790                C = pred.shape[1]
1791                if prev_state.shape[1] > C:
1792                    state0_norm = prev_state[:, -C:]
1793                else:
1794                    state0_norm = prev_state
1795                state1_norm = pred
1796
1797                # 2. Denormalize to physical units (PV loss expects physical)
1798                st0 = state0_norm * torch.sqrt(self.inp_var) + self.inp_mean
1799                st1 = state1_norm * torch.sqrt(self.inp_var) + self.inp_mean
1800                
1801                # 3. Compute U, V, Zeta, H for both steps
1802                # state: channels 0=h, 1=z, 2=d
1803                
1804                pv_loss = 0.0
1805                bs = pred.shape[0]
1806                for i in range(bs):
1807                    # Step 0
1808                    sp0 = self.solver.grid2spec(st0[i])
1809                    uv0 = self.solver.getuv(sp0[1:]) # (2, H, W)
1810                    u0, v0 = uv0[0], uv0[1]
1811                    z0 = st0[i, 1]
1812                    h0 = st0[i, 0]
1813                    
1814                    # Step 1
1815                    sp1 = self.solver.grid2spec(st1[i])
1816                    uv1 = self.solver.getuv(sp1[1:])
1817                    u1, v1 = uv1[0], uv1[1]
1818                    z1 = st1[i, 1]
1819                    h1 = st1[i, 0]
1820                    
1821                    loss_i, _, _ = self.pv_loss_fn(
1822                        u0.unsqueeze(0), v0.unsqueeze(0), z0.unsqueeze(0), None, h0.unsqueeze(0),
1823                        u1.unsqueeze(0), v1.unsqueeze(0), z1.unsqueeze(0), None, h1.unsqueeze(0)
1824                    )
1825                    pv_loss += loss_i
1826                
1827                pv_loss = pv_loss / bs
1828                self.last_spectral_pv_loss += pv_loss.item() * step_mult
1829                total = total + self.spectral_pv_lambda * pv_loss
1830
1831            # Spectral Mass Loss
1832            if self.mass_lambda > 0 and self.mass_loss_fn is not None and prev_state is not None:
1833                # 1. Prepare states
1834                C = pred.shape[1]
1835                if prev_state.shape[1] > C:
1836                    state0_norm = prev_state[:, -C:]
1837                else:
1838                    state0_norm = prev_state
1839                state1_norm = pred
1840
1841                # 2. Denormalize to physical units
1842                st0 = state0_norm * torch.sqrt(self.inp_var) + self.inp_mean
1843                st1 = state1_norm * torch.sqrt(self.inp_var) + self.inp_mean
1844                
1845                mass_loss = 0.0
1846                bs = pred.shape[0]
1847                for i in range(bs):
1848                    # Step 0
1849                    sp0 = self.solver.grid2spec(st0[i])
1850                    uv0 = self.solver.getuv(sp0[1:]) # (2, H, W)
1851                    u0, v0 = uv0[0], uv0[1]
1852                    h0 = st0[i, 0]
1853                    
1854                    # Step 1
1855                    sp1 = self.solver.grid2spec(st1[i])
1856                    uv1 = self.solver.getuv(sp1[1:])
1857                    u1, v1 = uv1[0], uv1[1]
1858                    h1 = st1[i, 0]
1859                    
1860                    loss_i, _, _ = self.mass_loss_fn(
1861                        u0.unsqueeze(0), v0.unsqueeze(0), h0.unsqueeze(0),
1862                        u1.unsqueeze(0), v1.unsqueeze(0), h1.unsqueeze(0)
1863                    )
1864                    mass_loss += loss_i
1865                
1866                mass_loss = mass_loss / bs
1867                self.last_mass_loss += mass_loss.item() * step_mult
1868                total = total + self.mass_lambda * mass_loss
1869
1870            # Time derivative second difference loss
1871            if self.time_diff2_lambda > 0 and prev_state is not None and prev_prev_state is not None and prev_target is not None and prev_prev_target is not None:
1872                # Extract last frame filters are needed if history is stacked (n_history > 1)
1873                
1874                # Handling stacked history (B, n*C, H, W) vs single (B, C, H, W)
1875                C = pred.shape[1]
1876                
1877                # Helper to extract last step from history stack
1878                def extract_last(state):
1879                    if state.shape[1] > C:
1880                        return state[:, -C:]
1881                    return state
1882
1883                curr = pred
1884                prev = extract_last(prev_state)
1885                prev_prev = extract_last(prev_prev_state)
1886                
1887                curr_tar = target
1888                prev_tar = extract_last(prev_target)
1889                prev_prev_tar = extract_last(prev_prev_target)
1890
1891                # Estimated Second Time Derivatives
1892                # u_{t} - 2u_{t-1} + u_{t-2}
1893                diff2_pred = curr - 2 * prev + prev_prev
1894                diff2_gt = curr_tar - 2 * prev_tar + prev_prev_tar
1895                
1896                # Match the acceleration
1897                loss_diff2 = self.l2_fn(diff2_pred, diff2_gt)
1898                
1899                self.last_time_diff2_loss += loss_diff2.item() * step_mult
1900                total = total + self.time_diff2_lambda * loss_diff2
1901
1902            if step == 0:
1903                total = total * self.first_step_lambda
1904                
1905            return total
1906    
1907    # Initialize CCC loss if needed
1908    ccc_loss_fn = CCCLoss().to(device) if ccc_lambda > 0 else None
1909    
1910    loss_fn = CombinedLoss(
1911        l2_fn=l2_loss_fn, 
1912        lap_fn=lap_loss_fn, 
1913        cons_fn=cons_loss_fn,
1914        ccc_fn=ccc_loss_fn,
1915        spectral_fn=spectral_loss_fn,
1916        gradient_fn=gradient_loss_fn,
1917        l2_lambda=l2_lambda, 
1918        lap_lambda=laplacian_lambda,
1919        cons_lambda=conservation_lambda,
1920        ccc_lambda=ccc_lambda,
1921        spectral_lambda=args.spectral_lambda,
1922        gradient_lambda=args.gradient_lambda,
1923        vorticity_lambda=vorticity_lambda,
1924        uv_lambda=uv_lambda,
1925        spectral_pv_lambda=args.spectral_pv_lambda,
1926        mass_lambda=args.mass_lambda,
1927        time_diff2_lambda=args.time_diff2_lambda,
1928        first_step_lambda=first_step_lambda,
1929        first_step_only_l2_lap_uv=first_step_only_l2_lap_uv,
1930        solver=dataset.solver,
1931        inp_mean=dataset.inp_mean,
1932        inp_var=dataset.inp_var,
1933        u_std=u_std,
1934        v_std=v_std,
1935        device=device
1936    ).to(device)
1937    
1938    loss_desc = f"{l2_lambda} * L2"
1939    if vorticity_lambda != 1.0:
1940        loss_desc += f" (vorticity weight={vorticity_lambda})"
1941    if laplacian_lambda > 0:
1942        loss_desc += f" + {laplacian_lambda} * Laplacian"
1943    if conservation_lambda > 0:
1944        loss_desc += f" + {conservation_lambda} * Conservation"
1945    if ccc_lambda > 0:
1946        loss_desc += f" + {ccc_lambda} * CCC"
1947    if args.spectral_lambda > 0:
1948        loss_desc += f" + {args.spectral_lambda} * Spectral (l>{args.spectral_lmin}, k={args.spectral_decay})"
1949    if args.gradient_lambda > 0:
1950        loss_desc += f" + {args.gradient_lambda} * Gradient"
1951    if uv_lambda > 0:
1952        loss_desc += f" + {uv_lambda} * UV_Recon"
1953    if args.spectral_pv_lambda > 0:
1954        loss_desc += f" + {args.spectral_pv_lambda} * Spectral_PV"
1955    if args.mass_lambda > 0:
1956        loss_desc += f" + {args.mass_lambda} * Spectral_Mass"
1957    if args.time_diff2_lambda > 0:
1958        loss_desc += f" + {args.time_diff2_lambda} * Time_Diff2"
1959    print(f"Using loss: {loss_desc}")
1960
1961    # dictionary for logging the metrics
1962    metrics = {}
1963    metrics_fns = {
1964        "L2 error": L2LossS2(nlat=nlat, nlon=nlon, grid=grid).to(device=device),
1965        "L1 error": L1LossS2(nlat=nlat, nlon=nlon, grid=grid).to(device=device),
1966        "W11 error": W11LossS2(nlat=nlat, nlon=nlon, grid=grid).to(device=device),
1967    }
1968
1969    # iterate over models and train each model
1970    for model_name, model_handle in models.items():
1971
1972        # Initialize wandb at the start of each model to capture entire log
1973        if logging and wandb is not None and train:
1974            wandb_kwargs = dict(
1975                project=project_str,
1976                group=model_name,
1977                name=model_name + "_" + str(time.time()),
1978                config={**model_handle.keywords, "PBS_JOBID": PBS_JOBID},
1979                settings=wandb.Settings(console="wrap"),
1980            )
1981            if args.wandb_run_id:
1982                wandb_kwargs["id"] = args.wandb_run_id
1983                wandb_kwargs["resume"] = "must"
1984            run = wandb.init(**wandb_kwargs)
1985            print(f"PBS Job ID: {PBS_JOBID}", flush=True)
1986        else:
1987            run = None
1988
1989        model = model_handle().to(device)
1990        
1991        # Check if model has a fixed n_history preference (from registry)
1992        # If not, apply the command-line argument
1993        if not hasattr(model, 'n_history'):
1994            model.n_history = n_history
1995        
1996        # Get n_history from model attribute (now guaranteed to exist)
1997        current_n_history = getattr(model, 'n_history', 1)
1998        
1999        if current_n_history > 1:
2000            print(f"Multi-history model detected: n_history={current_n_history}")
2001            # Update dataset with correct n_history for training data
2002            dataset.n_history = current_n_history
2003        else:
2004             # Ensure dataset uses 1 if model is single-step
2005             dataset.n_history = 1
2006
2007        print(model)
2008
2009        metrics[model_name] = {}
2010
2011        num_params = count_parameters(model)
2012        print(f"number of trainable params: {num_params}")
2013        metrics[model_name]["num_params"] = num_params
2014
2015        exp_dir = os.path.join(root_path, model_name)
2016        if not os.path.isdir(exp_dir):
2017            os.makedirs(exp_dir, exist_ok=True)
2018            
2019        # Attach best checkpoint path to model (derive from checkpoint_name to preserve lineages)
2020        if checkpoint_name and checkpoint_name != "checkpoint.pt":
2021            base_name = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(checkpoint_name))[0]
2022            best_name = f"best_{base_name}.pt"
2023            recent_name = f"recent_{base_name}.pt"
2024        else:
2025            best_name = "best_checkpoint.pt"
2026            recent_name = "recent_checkpoint.pt"
2027        model.best_checkpoint_path = os.path.join(exp_dir, best_name)
2028        model.recent_checkpoint_path = os.path.join(exp_dir, recent_name)
2029
2030        if load_checkpoint:
2031            print(f"Loading checkpoint: {checkpoint_name}")
2032            if device.type == "cpu":
2033                model.load_state_dict(torch.load(os.path.join(exp_dir, checkpoint_name), map_location=torch.device("cpu")), strict=False)
2034            else:
2035                model.load_state_dict(torch.load(os.path.join(exp_dir, checkpoint_name)), strict=False)
2036
2037        # run the training
2038        if train:
2039            # Setup scratch directory for checkpoints, e.g. /glade/derecho/scratch/$USER/TH_SWE_output/model_name/checkpoints
2040            scratch_root = f"/glade/derecho/scratch/{os.environ['USER']}/TH_SWE_output"
2041            model_scratch_dir = os.path.join(scratch_root, model_name, "checkpoints")
2042            if not os.path.exists(model_scratch_dir):
2043                os.makedirs(model_scratch_dir, exist_ok=True)
2044            if logging:
2045                print(f"Saving per-epoch checkpoints to: {model_scratch_dir}")
2046
2047            # Parse learning rate - use first value for pretraining
2048            base_learning_rate = parse_list_arg(learning_rate, dtype=float)[0]
2049            optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=base_learning_rate)
2050            scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.ReduceLROnPlateau(optimizer, "min")
2051            gscaler = torch.GradScaler("cuda", enabled=(amp_mode == "fp16"))
2052
2053            start_time = time.time()
2054
2055            if logging:
2056                print(f"Training {model_name}, single step")
2057
2058            # ── Periodic plot callback ──────────────────────────────────────
2059            plot_callback = None
2060            if plotting_dt > 0 and wandb is not None:
2061                _last_plot_time = [0.0]  # mutable container for closure
2062                _plot_nics = 3
2063                _plot_steps = min(92, validate_steps)  # shorter than full validation
2064                _dt_minutes = (dt_solver * nsteps) / 60.0  # minutes per ML step
2065
2066                def generate_and_log_plots(model, epoch_label=""):
2067                    """Run a quick rollout and log plots to wandb (if enough time has passed)."""
2068                    now = time.time()
2069                    if now - _last_plot_time[0] < plotting_dt:
2070                        return  # too soon
2071                    _last_plot_time[0] = now
2072
2073                    model.eval()
2074                    print(f"  [plot_callback] Generating plots ({_plot_nics} ICs, {_plot_steps} steps)...")
2075                    plot_start = time.time()
2076
2077                    try:
2078                        # Quick rollout (same logic as autoregressive_inference, but lighter)
2079                        all_preds = []
2080                        all_truth = []
2081                        inp_mean = dataset.inp_mean
2082                        inp_var = dataset.inp_var
2083
2084                        with torch.inference_mode():
2085                            for iic in range(_plot_nics):
2086                                use_precomputed_truth = False
2087                                states_chunk = None
2088
2089                                if iic == 0:
2090                                    ic = dataset.solver.galewsky_initial_condition()
2091                                else:
2092                                    if precomputed_ds is not None:
2093                                        use_precomputed_truth = True
2094                                        max_start = precomputed_ds.T_total - 1 - _plot_steps
2095                                        if max_start < 0:
2096                                             # Should not happen if _plot_steps is reasonable
2097                                             ic = dataset.solver.random_initial_condition(mach=ic_mach)
2098                                             use_precomputed_truth = False
2099                                        else:
2100                                            traj_idx = torch.randint(0, precomputed_ds.N, (1,)).item()
2101                                            t_idx = torch.randint(0, max_start + 1, (1,)).item()
2102                                            states_chunk = torch.from_numpy(precomputed_ds.states[traj_idx, t_idx : t_idx + _plot_steps + 1])
2103                                            states_chunk = states_chunk.float().to(device)
2104                                            state0_grid = states_chunk[0]
2105                                    else:
2106                                        ic = dataset.solver.random_initial_condition(mach=ic_mach)
2107
2108                                if not use_precomputed_truth:
2109                                    state0_grid = dataset.solver.spec2grid(ic)
2110                                    
2111                                state0 = (state0_grid - inp_mean) / torch.sqrt(inp_var)
2112                                state0 = state0.unsqueeze(0)  # (1, C, H, W)
2113                                
2114                                if not use_precomputed_truth:
2115                                    uspec = ic.clone()
2116
2117                                ic_preds = [state0[0].detach().cpu()]
2118                                ic_truth = [state0[0].detach().cpu()]
2119
2120                                # Initialize history buffer
2121                                if n_history > 1:
2122                                    history_buffer = [state0.clone() for _ in range(n_history)]
2123
2124                                # ML rollout
2125                                prd = state0
2126                                for i in range(1, _plot_steps + 1):
2127                                    if n_history > 1:
2128                                        model_input = torch.cat(history_buffer, dim=1)
2129                                    else:
2130                                        model_input = prd
2131                                    prd = model(model_input)
2132                                    if n_history > 1:
2133                                        history_buffer = history_buffer[1:] + [prd.clone()]
2134                                    ic_preds.append(prd[0].detach().cpu())
2135
2136                                # Classical solver rollout or Precomputed Truth
2137                                if use_precomputed_truth:
2138                                    for i in range(1, _plot_steps + 1):
2139                                        ref_phys = states_chunk[i]
2140                                        ref = (ref_phys - inp_mean) / torch.sqrt(inp_var)
2141                                        ic_truth.append(ref.detach().cpu())
2142                                else:
2143                                    for i in range(1, _plot_steps + 1):
2144                                        uspec = dataset.solver.timestep(uspec, nsteps)
2145                                        ref = (dataset.solver.spec2grid(uspec) - inp_mean) / torch.sqrt(inp_var)
2146                                        ic_truth.append(ref.detach().cpu())
2147
2148                                all_preds.append(torch.stack(ic_preds))
2149                                all_truth.append(torch.stack(ic_truth))
2150
2151                        # Stack: (nics, steps, C, H, W)
2152                        preds_np = torch.stack(all_preds).numpy()
2153                        truth_np = torch.stack(all_truth).numpy()
2154
2155                        log_dict = {}
2156
2157                        # Rollout snapshots for each channel
2158                        for ch in range(min(3, preds_np.shape[2])):
2159                            fig = plot_rollout_snapshots(
2160                                preds_np, truth_np, channel=ch,
2161                                n_snapshots=6,
2162                            )
2163                            log_dict[f"rollout_{['height','vorticity','divergence'][ch]}"] = wandb.Image(fig)
2164                            plt.close(fig)
2165
2166                        # RMSE vs lead time
2167                        fig = plot_rmse_vs_leadtime(
2168                            preds_np, truth_np,
2169                            channels=list(range(min(3, preds_np.shape[2]))),
2170                            dt_minutes=_dt_minutes
2171                        )
2172                        log_dict["rmse_vs_leadtime"] = wandb.Image(fig)
2173                        plt.close(fig)
2174
2175                        # Variance vs lead time
2176                        fig = plot_variance_vs_leadtime(
2177                            preds_np, truth_np,
2178                            channels=list(range(min(3, preds_np.shape[2]))),
2179                            dt_minutes=_dt_minutes
2180                        )
2181                        log_dict["variance_vs_leadtime"] = wandb.Image(fig)
2182                        plt.close(fig)
2183
2184                        if wandb.run is not None:
2185                            wandb.log(log_dict, commit=False)
2186
2187                        plot_time = time.time() - plot_start
2188                        print(f"  [plot_callback] Done in {plot_time:.1f}s")
2189
2190                    except Exception as e:
2191                        print(f"  [plot_callback] Error: {e}")
2192                    finally:
2193                        model.train()
2194                
2195                # Assign the function to the variable!
2196                plot_callback = generate_and_log_plots
2197
2198            train_model(
2199                model,
2200                dataloader,
2201                loss_fn,
2202                metrics_fns,
2203                optimizer,
2204                gscaler,
2205                scheduler,
2206                nepochs=pretrain_epochs,
2207                amp_mode=amp_mode,
2208                log_grads=log_grads,
2209                logging=logging,
2210                device=device,
2211                n_history=n_history,
2212                noise_injection_sigma=args.noise_injection_sigma,
2213                noise_injection_rate=args.noise_injection_rate,
2214                plot_callback=plot_callback,
2215                scratch_dir=model_scratch_dir,
2216                time_diff2_lambda=args.time_diff2_lambda,
2217            )
2218
2219            # Parse finetuning arguments into lists
2220            finetune_epochs_list = parse_list_arg(finetune_epochs, dtype=int)
2221            finetune_steps_list = parse_list_arg(finetune_steps, dtype=int)
2222            learning_rate_list = parse_list_arg(learning_rate, dtype=float)
2223            
2224            # Determine if we're doing multi-phase finetuning
2225            n_finetune_phases = max(len(finetune_epochs_list), len(finetune_steps_list))
2226            has_finetune = any(e > 0 for e in finetune_epochs_list) and n_finetune_phases > 0
2227            
2228            if has_finetune:
2229                # Broadcast single values to match number of phases
2230                if len(finetune_epochs_list) == 1 and n_finetune_phases > 1:
2231                    finetune_epochs_list = finetune_epochs_list * n_finetune_phases
2232                if len(finetune_steps_list) == 1 and n_finetune_phases > 1:
2233                    finetune_steps_list = finetune_steps_list * n_finetune_phases
2234                
2235                # Handle learning rate list:
2236                # - If single LR with pretrain > 0: use for pretrain, apply 0.1x for all finetune phases (backward compat)
2237                # - If single LR with pretrain == 0: apply 0.1x for all finetune phases (backward compat)
2238                # - If LR list length == n_finetune_phases: use directly for finetuning (no 0.1x)
2239                # - If LR list length == n_finetune_phases + 1 and pretrain > 0: first for pretrain, rest for finetuning
2240                if len(learning_rate_list) == 1:
2241                    # Backward compatibility: single LR, apply 0.1x factor for finetuning
2242                    finetune_lrs = [0.1 * learning_rate_list[0]] * n_finetune_phases
2243                elif len(learning_rate_list) == n_finetune_phases:
2244                    # Explicit LRs for finetuning phases only (no 0.1x)
2245                    finetune_lrs = learning_rate_list
2246                elif len(learning_rate_list) == n_finetune_phases + 1 and pretrain_epochs > 0:
2247                    # First LR for pretrain (already used above), rest for finetuning
2248                    finetune_lrs = learning_rate_list[1:]
2249                else:
2250                    raise ValueError(f"learning_rate list length ({len(learning_rate_list)}) must be 1, {n_finetune_phases}, or {n_finetune_phases + 1}")
2251                
2252                # Run each finetuning phase
2253                for phase_idx in range(n_finetune_phases):
2254                    phase_epochs = finetune_epochs_list[phase_idx]
2255                    phase_steps = finetune_steps_list[phase_idx]
2256                    phase_lr = finetune_lrs[phase_idx]
2257                    
2258                    if phase_epochs == 0:
2259                        continue
2260                    
2261                    nfuture = phase_steps - 1
2262
2263                    if logging:
2264                        print(f"Finetuning phase {phase_idx + 1}/{n_finetune_phases}: {phase_epochs} epochs, {phase_steps} steps, lr={phase_lr}")
2265
2266                    optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=phase_lr)
2267                    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.ReduceLROnPlateau(optimizer, "min")
2268                    gscaler = torch.GradScaler(enabled=(amp_mode != "none"))
2269                    
2270                    # Rebuild DataLoader so finetuning workers see the new nfuture window.
2271                    # Mutating dataset.nfuture in place would leave persistent workers
2272                    # with a stale window size, causing them to silently return
2273                    # single-step targets instead of trajectories.
2274                    if precomputed_ds is not None:
2275                        dataloader = _make_precomputed_loader(nfuture_val=nfuture)
2276                    else:
2277                        dataloader.dataset.nfuture = nfuture
2278                    
2279
2280                    train_model(
2281                        model,
2282                        dataloader,
2283                        loss_fn,
2284                        metrics_fns,
2285                        optimizer,
2286                        gscaler,
2287                        scheduler,
2288                        nepochs=phase_epochs,
2289                        nfuture=nfuture,
2290                        amp_mode=amp_mode,
2291                        log_grads=log_grads, 
2292                        logging=logging,
2293                        device=device,
2294                        n_history=n_history,
2295                        noise_injection_sigma=args.noise_injection_sigma,
2296                        noise_injection_rate=args.noise_injection_rate,
2297                        plot_callback=plot_callback,
2298                        scratch_dir=model_scratch_dir,
2299                        time_diff2_lambda=args.time_diff2_lambda,
2300                    )
2301
2302                    
2303                    # Save checkpoint after each phase
2304                    phase_checkpoint = os.path.join(exp_dir, f"checkpoint_phase{phase_idx + 1}_AR{phase_steps}.pt")
2305                    torch.save(model.state_dict(), phase_checkpoint)
2306                    torch.save(model.state_dict(), os.path.join(exp_dir, "checkpoint.pt"))
2307                    if logging:
2308                        print(f"Saved phase checkpoint: {phase_checkpoint}")
2309                    
2310                    # Run validation rollout after each phase (if validate_steps > 0)
2311                    if validate_steps > 0 and nics > 0:
2312                        phase_tag = f"AR{phase_steps}"
2313                        if logging:
2314                            print(f"Running validation rollout for phase {phase_idx + 1} ({phase_tag})...")
2315                        with torch.inference_mode():
2316                            losses, _, _, _ = autoregressive_inference(
2317                                model, dataset, loss_fn, metrics_fns, 
2318                                os.path.join(exp_dir, "figures"), 
2319                                nsteps=nsteps, autoreg_steps=validate_steps, 
2320                                nics=nics, device=device, 
2321                                run_tag=phase_tag, n_history=n_history,
2322                                precomputed_dataset=precomputed_ds
2323                            )
2324                            # Stability check
2325                            is_stable = torch.isfinite(losses).all().item() and (losses.mean() < 1e6)
2326                            print(f"Phase {phase_idx + 1} ({phase_tag}) Stability Check ({validate_steps} steps): {'PASSED' if is_stable else 'FAILED'}")
2327                
2328                # Reset nfuture back to 0 — rebuild loader so workers are re-forked
2329                # with the correct single-step window for any subsequent pretraining.
2330                if precomputed_ds is not None:
2331                    dataloader = _make_precomputed_loader(nfuture_val=0)
2332                else:
2333                    dataloader.dataset.nfuture = 0
2334
2335            # Curriculum rollout training phase (optional)
2336            if curriculum_epochs > 0:
2337                if logging:
2338                    print(f"Curriculum rollout training {model_name}, {curriculum_epochs} epochs")
2339                
2340                # Parse curriculum schedule
2341                if curriculum_max_steps is not None:
2342                    max_steps_schedule = [int(x) for x in curriculum_max_steps.split(",")]
2343                else:
2344                    max_steps_schedule = None  # Use default linear ramp
2345                
2346                # Use 0.1x of base learning rate for curriculum (similar to old finetune behavior)
2347                curriculum_lr = 0.1 * parse_list_arg(learning_rate, dtype=float)[0]
2348                optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=curriculum_lr)
2349                scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.ReduceLROnPlateau(optimizer, "min")
2350                gscaler = torch.GradScaler("cuda", enabled=(amp_mode == "fp16"))
2351                
2352                train_curriculum_rollout(
2353                    model,
2354                    dataset,
2355                    loss_fn,
2356                    optimizer,
2357                    gscaler,
2358                    scheduler,
2359                    nepochs=curriculum_epochs,
2360                    num_examples=num_examples,
2361                    batch_size=batch_size,
2362                    amp_mode=amp_mode,
2363                    max_steps_schedule=max_steps_schedule,
2364                    logging=logging,
2365                    device=device,
2366                    n_history=n_history,
2367                    noise_injection_sigma=args.noise_injection_sigma,
2368                    noise_injection_rate=args.noise_injection_rate,
2369                    plot_callback=plot_callback,
2370                    scratch_dir=model_scratch_dir,
2371                )
2372
2373            training_time = time.time() - start_time
2374
2375            torch.save(model.state_dict(), os.path.join(exp_dir, "checkpoint.pt"))
2376            
2377            # Save a tagged checkpoint if run_tag is provided (preserves each stage)
2378            if run_tag:
2379                tagged_checkpoint = os.path.join(exp_dir, f"checkpoint_{run_tag}.pt")
2380                torch.save(model.state_dict(), tagged_checkpoint)
2381                print(f"Saved tagged checkpoint: {tagged_checkpoint}")
2382
2383        # set seed
2384        torch.manual_seed(333)
2385        torch.cuda.manual_seed(333)
2386
2387        # run validation
2388        print(f"Validating {model_name}")
2389        with torch.inference_mode():
2390            losses, metric_results, model_times, solver_times = autoregressive_inference(
2391                model, dataset, loss_fn, metrics_fns, os.path.join(exp_dir, "figures"), 
2392                nsteps=nsteps, autoreg_steps=validate_steps, nics=nics, device=device, 
2393                run_tag=run_tag, n_history=n_history,
2394                precomputed_dataset=precomputed_ds
2395            )
2396
2397            # Stability Check
2398            if nics > 0:
2399                is_stable = torch.isfinite(losses).all().item() and (losses.mean() < 1e6) 
2400                print(f"Stability Check ({validate_steps} steps): {'PASSED' if is_stable else 'FAILED'}")
2401                metrics[model_name]["stable"] = is_stable
2402
2403                # compute statistics
2404                metrics[model_name]["loss mean"] = torch.mean(losses).item()
2405                metrics[model_name]["loss std"] = torch.std(losses).item()
2406                if isinstance(model_times, list):
2407                     model_times = torch.tensor(model_times)
2408                if isinstance(solver_times, list):
2409                     solver_times = torch.tensor(solver_times)
2410                metrics[model_name]["model time mean"] = torch.mean(model_times).item()
2411                metrics[model_name]["model time std"] = torch.std(model_times).item()
2412                metrics[model_name]["solver time mean"] = torch.mean(solver_times).item()
2413                metrics[model_name]["solver time std"] = torch.std(solver_times).item()
2414                for metric in metric_results:
2415                    metrics[model_name][metric + " mean"] = torch.mean(metric_results[metric]).item()
2416                    metrics[model_name][metric + " std"] = torch.std(metric_results[metric]).item()
2417
2418            if train:
2419                metrics[model_name]["training_time"] = training_time
2420
2421        # Finish wandb run after validation completes to capture entire log
2422        if run is not None:
2423            run.finish()
2424
2425    # output metrics to data frame
2426    df = pd.DataFrame(metrics)
2427    if not os.path.isdir(os.path.join(root_path, "output_data")):
2428        os.makedirs(os.path.join(root_path, "output_data"), exist_ok=True)
2429    df.to_pickle(os.path.join(root_path, "output_data", "metrics.pkl"))
2430
2431
2432if __name__ == "__main__":
2433    import torch.multiprocessing as mp
2434
2435    mp.set_start_method("forkserver", force=True)
2436    _log_startup("Set multiprocessing start method")
2437    if wandb is not None:
2438        _log_startup("Starting wandb.login()...")
2439        wandb.login()
2440        _log_startup("wandb.login() complete")
2441
2442    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
2443    parser.add_argument(
2444        "--root_path", default=os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "checkpoints"), type=str, help="Override the path where checkpoints and run information are stored"
2445    )
2446    parser.add_argument("--pretrain_epochs", default=15, type=int, help="Number of pretraining epochs.")
2447    parser.add_argument("--finetune_epochs", default="0", type=str, help="Number of fine-tuning epochs. Can be comma-separated for multi-phase (e.g., '12,6,6').")
2448    parser.add_argument("--batch_size", default=4, type=int, help="Switch for overriding batch size in the configuration file.")
2449    parser.add_argument("--learning_rate", default="1e-4", type=str, help="Learning rate. Can be comma-separated for multi-phase (e.g., '1e-3,5e-4,2e-4').")
2450    parser.add_argument("--resume", action="store_true", help="Reload checkpoints.")
2451    parser.add_argument("--amp_mode", default="none", type=str, choices=["none", "bf16", "fp16"], help="Switch to enable AMP.")
2452    parser.add_argument("--validate_steps", default=96, type=int, help="Number of autoregressive steps for validation.")
2453    parser.add_argument("--nics", default=50, type=int, help="Number of initial conditions for validation.")
2454    parser.add_argument("--checkpoint_name", default="checkpoint.pt", type=str, help="Name of the checkpoint file to load.")
2455    parser.add_argument("--finetune_steps", default="2", type=str, help="Number of autoregressive steps for fine-tuning. Can be comma-separated for multi-phase (e.g., '2,6,12').")
2456    parser.add_argument("--curriculum_epochs", default=0, type=int, help="Number of curriculum rollout training epochs (0 to disable).")
2457    parser.add_argument("--curriculum_max_steps", default=None, type=str, help="Comma-separated schedule for max rollout steps per epoch, e.g., '1,2,4,8,16'.")
2458    parser.add_argument("--seed", default=None, type=int, help="Random seed for reproducibility (default: random).")
2459    parser.add_argument("--l2_lambda", default=1.0, type=float, help="Weight for L2 loss term (default 1.0).")
2460    parser.add_argument("--laplacian_lambda", default=0.0, type=float, help="Weight for Laplacian loss term (0 to disable).")
2461    parser.add_argument("--conservation_lambda", default=0.0, type=float, help="Weight for conservation loss term (0 to disable).")
2462    parser.add_argument("--ccc_lambda", default=0.0, type=float, help="Weight for CCC (Concordance Correlation Coefficient) loss term (0 to disable).")
2463    parser.add_argument("--spectral_lambda", default=0.0, type=float, help="Weight for Spectral loss term (0 to disable).")
2464    parser.add_argument("--spectral_lmin", default=0, type=int, help="Minimum wavenumber l to penalize in spectral loss.")
2465    parser.add_argument("--spectral_lmax", default=None, type=int, help="Maximum wavenumber l to penalize in spectral loss (default: Nyquist).")
2466    parser.add_argument("--spectral_decay", default=0.0, type=float, help="Exponent k for weighting spectral loss by l^k.")
2467    parser.add_argument("--gradient_lambda", default=0.0, type=float, help="Weight for Gradient (H1) loss term (0 to disable).")
2468    parser.add_argument("--vorticity_lambda", type=float, default=1.0, help="Weight for vorticity channel in L2 loss")
2469    parser.add_argument("--uv_lambda", type=float, default=0.0, help="Weight for UV reconstruction loss")
2470    parser.add_argument("--spectral_pv_lambda", type=float, default=0.0, help="Weight for Spectral PV loss")
2471    parser.add_argument("--mass_lambda", type=float, default=0.0, help="Weight for mass conservation loss")
2472    parser.add_argument("--run_tag", type=str, default=None, help="Optional run tag for logging")
2473    parser.add_argument("--num_examples", type=int, default=256, help="Number of training examples per epoch")
2474    parser.add_argument("--noise_injection_sigma", type=float, default=0.0, help="Standard deviation of Gaussian noise to inject into inputs")
2475    parser.add_argument("--noise_injection_rate", type=float, default=0.0, help="Probability of duplicating and corrupting samples in a batch")
2476    parser.add_argument("--ic_mach", type=float, default=0.2, help="Mach number for initial conditions (default: 0.2)")
2477    parser.add_argument("--ic_llimit", type=int, default=25, help="Maximum spectral wavenumber for IC noise (default: 25).")
2478    parser.add_argument("--stochastic_ic_llimit", action="store_true", help="If set, randomly sample ic_llimit for each initial condition.")
2479    parser.add_argument("--stochastic_ic_mach", action="store_true", help="If set, randomly sample uniform [0, 2*ic_mach] for each initial condition.")
2480    parser.add_argument("--wandb_run_id", default=None, type=str, help="W&B run ID to resume (8-char ID from the W&B dashboard URL).")
2481    parser.add_argument("--plotting_dt", default=0, type=int, help="Seconds between periodic plot logging to wandb (0 to disable).")
2482    parser.add_argument("--ic_spinup_max", default=0, type=int, help="Max number of solver macro-steps to advance each IC before training (0 to disable). Actual spinup per sample is randomly sampled from 0..max.")
2483    parser.add_argument("--time_diff2_lambda", default=0.0, type=float, help="Weight for second derivative (time) loss term.")
2484    parser.add_argument("--n_history", default=1, type=int, help="Number of past timesteps to use as input (default: 1).")
2485    parser.add_argument("--precomputed_db", type=str, default=None,
2486                        help="Path to pre-generated Zarr training database directory. "
2487                             "If set, uses precomputed data instead of on-the-fly solver generation.")
2488    parser.add_argument("--first_step_lambda", default=1.0, type=float, help="weight multiplier for the total loss of the very first step of a rollout")
2489    parser.add_argument("--first_step_only_l2_lap_uv", action="store_true", help="If enabled, ignores L2, Laplacian, and UV losses for all steps > 0.")
2490    parser.add_argument("--checkpoint_activation", action="store_true", help="Enable gradient checkpointing for memory savings.")
2491    
2492    args = parser.parse_args()
2493
2494    # main(train=False, load_checkpoint=True, enable_amp=False, log_grads=0)
2495    main(
2496        root_path=args.root_path,
2497        pretrain_epochs=args.pretrain_epochs,
2498        finetune_epochs=args.finetune_epochs,
2499        batch_size=args.batch_size,
2500        learning_rate=args.learning_rate,
2501        train=(int(args.pretrain_epochs) > 0 or any(int(x) > 0 for x in args.finetune_epochs.split(",")) or int(args.curriculum_epochs) > 0),
2502        load_checkpoint=args.resume,
2503        checkpoint_name=args.checkpoint_name,
2504        amp_mode=args.amp_mode,
2505        log_grads=0,
2506        validate_steps=args.validate_steps,
2507        nics=args.nics,
2508        finetune_steps=args.finetune_steps,
2509        curriculum_epochs=args.curriculum_epochs,
2510        curriculum_max_steps=args.curriculum_max_steps,
2511        seed=args.seed,
2512        l2_lambda=args.l2_lambda,
2513        laplacian_lambda=args.laplacian_lambda,
2514        conservation_lambda=args.conservation_lambda,
2515        ccc_lambda=args.ccc_lambda,
2516        vorticity_lambda=args.vorticity_lambda,
2517        uv_lambda=args.uv_lambda,
2518        spectral_pv_lambda=args.spectral_pv_lambda,
2519        mass_lambda=args.mass_lambda,
2520        run_tag=args.run_tag,
2521        num_examples=args.num_examples,
2522        ic_mach=args.ic_mach,
2523        ic_llimit=args.ic_llimit,
2524        stochastic_ic_llimit=args.stochastic_ic_llimit,
2525        stochastic_ic_mach=args.stochastic_ic_mach,
2526        plotting_dt=args.plotting_dt,
2527        ic_spinup_max=args.ic_spinup_max,
2528        time_diff2_lambda=args.time_diff2_lambda,
2529        n_history=args.n_history,
2530        precomputed_db_path=args.precomputed_db,
2531        first_step_lambda=args.first_step_lambda,
2532        first_step_only_l2_lap_uv=args.first_step_only_l2_lap_uv,
2533        checkpoint_activation=args.checkpoint_activation,
2534    )
PBS_JOBID = 'N/A'
def count_parameters(model): 
109def count_parameters(model):
110    return sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters() if p.requires_grad)
def log_weights_and_grads(model, iters=1): 
114def log_weights_and_grads(model, iters=1):
115    root_path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "weights_and_grads")
116
117    weights_and_grads_fname = os.path.join(root_path, f"weights_and_grads_step{iters:03d}.tar")
118    print(weights_and_grads_fname)
119
120    weights_dict = {k: v for k, v in model.named_parameters()}
121    grad_dict = {k: v.grad for k, v in model.named_parameters()}
122
123    store_dict = {"iteration": iters, "grads": grad_dict, "weights": weights_dict}
124    torch.save(store_dict, weights_and_grads_fname)
def gaussian_bump_initial_condition(solver): 
128def gaussian_bump_initial_condition(solver):
129    """
130    Generate a static 'lifted height' initial condition.
131    Gaussian bump in geopotential height centered at 45N, 180E.
132    Zero vorticity and zero divergence.
133    """
134    device = solver.lap.device
135    ctype = torch.complex128 if solver.lap.dtype == torch.float64 else torch.complex64
136    
137    # Grid coordinates
138    # lats: (nlat,), lons: (nlon,)
139    lats = solver.lats.squeeze()
140    lons = solver.lons.squeeze()
141    Lats, Lons = torch.meshgrid(lats, lons, indexing='ij')
142    
143    # Bump parameters
144    lat0 = 45.0 * math.pi / 180.0
145    lon0 = math.pi # 180 degrees
146    # Use a finite radius for the Cosine Bell to ensure exact zero support
147    radius_bump = 40.0 * math.pi / 180.0 
148    amp = solver.hamp * 2.0 # Reduced amplitude 
149    
150    # Distance (Great circle central angle)
151    central_angle = torch.acos(
152        torch.clamp(
153            torch.sin(Lats) * math.sin(lat0) + 
154            torch.cos(Lats) * math.cos(lat0) * torch.cos(Lons - lon0), 
155            -1.0, 1.0
156        )
157    )
158    dist = central_angle
159    
160    # Cosine Bell: 0.5 * A * (1 + cos(pi * r / R)) for r < R
161    h_perturbation = torch.zeros_like(dist)
162    mask = dist < radius_bump
163    h_perturbation[mask] = 0.5 * amp * (1.0 + torch.cos(math.pi * dist[mask] / radius_bump))
164    
165    h_grid = solver.havg + h_perturbation
166    
167    # Scale by gravity (solver expects geopotential)
168    phi_grid = solver.gravity * h_grid
169    
170    # Convert to spectral
171    phi_spec = solver.grid2spec(phi_grid)
172    
173    # Full state: [Phi, Vort, Div]
174    uspec = torch.zeros(3, solver.lmax, solver.mmax, dtype=ctype, device=device)
175    uspec[0] = phi_spec
176    # Vorticity (1) and Divergence (2) remain zero
177    
178    return uspec

Generate a static 'lifted height' initial condition. Gaussian bump in geopotential height centered at 45N, 180E. Zero vorticity and zero divergence.

def autoregressive_inference( model, dataset, loss_fn, metrics_fns, path_root, nsteps, autoreg_steps=10, nskip=1, plot_channel=0, nics=10, device=device(type='cpu'), run_tag=None, n_history=1, precomputed_dataset=None): 
180def autoregressive_inference(
181    model,
182    dataset,
183    loss_fn,
184    metrics_fns,
185    path_root,
186    nsteps,
187    autoreg_steps=10,
188    nskip=1,
189    plot_channel=0,
190    nics=10,
191    device=torch.device("cpu"),
192    run_tag=None,
193    n_history=1,  # Number of past timesteps model expects (1=single-step, 3=AB3-like)
194    precomputed_dataset=None,
195):
196
197    model.eval()
198
199    if nics <= 0:
200        print("Skipping autoregressive inference (nics=0)")
201        return torch.tensor([]), {}, [], []
202
203    # make output
204    if not os.path.isdir(path_root):
205        os.makedirs(path_root, exist_ok=True)
206
207    # accumulation buffers for losses, metrics and runtimes
208    losses = torch.zeros(nics, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
209    metrics = {}
210    for metric in metrics_fns:
211        metrics[metric] = torch.zeros(nics, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
212    model_times = torch.zeros(nics, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
213    solver_times = torch.zeros(nics, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
214
215    # accumulation buffers for the power spectrum
216    prd_mean_coeffs = []
217    ref_mean_coeffs = []
218
219    # Note: We need to accumulate fields for NetCDF saving
220    # shape: (nics, steps, C, lat, lon)
221    all_preds = []
222    all_truth = []
223    
224    # New buffers for u and v components
225    all_preds_u = []
226    all_preds_v = []
227    all_truth_u = []
228    all_truth_v = []
229    
230    for iic in range(nics):
231        # Use Galewsky IC for first sample, Gaussian Bump for second, random for rest
232        use_precomputed_truth = False
233        states_chunk = None
234
235        if iic == 0:
236            ic = dataset.solver.galewsky_initial_condition()
237        elif iic == 1:
238            ic = gaussian_bump_initial_condition(dataset.solver)
239        else:
240            if precomputed_dataset is not None:
241                # Use precomputed trajectory
242                use_precomputed_truth = True
243                
244                # Sample random trajectory and start time
245                # Ensure we have enough future steps: t_start + autoreg_steps <= T_total - 1
246                # So max t_start = T_total - 1 - autoreg_steps
247                max_start = precomputed_dataset.T_total - 1 - autoreg_steps
248                
249                # If window is too large for trajectory, this might fail, but T_total should be large
250                if max_start < 0:
251                     raise ValueError(f"Trajectory length {precomputed_dataset.T_total} too short for validation rollout {autoreg_steps}")
252                
253                traj_idx = torch.randint(0, precomputed_dataset.N, (1,)).item()
254                t_idx = torch.randint(0, max_start + 1, (1,)).item()
255                
256                # Load chunk: [t_idx, t_idx + autoreg_steps + 1) -> length autoreg_steps + 1
257                # states are (N, T, C, H, W)
258                # We need to handle potential retries here too if Zarr is flaky, but dataset class has it.
259                # However we are accessing .states directly here.
260                # Let's trust Zarr combined with the earlier retry patch or just do a simple try/loop if needed.
261                # Since we are in validation, a crash is annoying but less critical than training loop. 
262                # We'll access via the dataset object if possible or just direct slice. 
263                # Direct slice is easiest given we need a custom window length.
264                
265                # shape: (autoreg_steps + 1, C, H, W)
266                states_chunk = torch.from_numpy(precomputed_dataset.states[traj_idx, t_idx : t_idx + autoreg_steps + 1])
267                states_chunk = states_chunk.float().to(device)
268                
269                # Initial state (step 0)
270                state0_grid = states_chunk[0] # (C, H, W)
271                
272                # Convert to spectral IC for model input compatibility (if model needs spectral)
273                # But here we just need Grid state for normalization
274                # We do need 'ic' (spectral) if we were running solver, but we aren't.
275                # However, consistent with below, we might want 'ic' for some reason? 
276                # Actually, 'uspec' is derived from 'ic'. 'uspec' is used for solver stepping.
277                # We won't step solver, so we don't strictly need 'ic' or 'uspec' for truth evolution.
278                # But we do need 'ic' if we want to calculate initial U/V using solver methods (grid2spec)
279                ic = dataset.solver.grid2spec(state0_grid)
280                
281            else:
282                ic = dataset.solver.random_initial_condition(mach=0.2)
283
284        inp_mean = dataset.inp_mean
285        inp_var = dataset.inp_var
286
287        # Initial Condition (step 0) - single normalized state (C, H, W)
288        if use_precomputed_truth:
289             # state0_grid already extracted
290             pass
291        else:
292             state0_grid = dataset.solver.spec2grid(ic)
293             
294        state0 = (state0_grid - inp_mean) / torch.sqrt(inp_var)
295        state0 = state0.unsqueeze(0)  # (1, C, H, W)
296        
297        if not use_precomputed_truth:
298            uspec = ic.clone()
299
300        # Storage for this IC
301        ic_preds = [state0[0].detach().cpu()]
302        ic_truth = [state0[0].detach().cpu()]
303        
304        # Calculate initial u/v for prediction (from ground truth IC)
305        # dataset.solver.getuv expects spectral vorticity and divergence
306        # ic is full spectral state (height, vorticity, divergence)
307        uv_ic = dataset.solver.getuv(ic[1:]) # (2, lat, lon)
308        ic_preds_u = [uv_ic[0].detach().cpu()]
309        ic_preds_v = [uv_ic[1].detach().cpu()]
310        ic_truth_u = [uv_ic[0].detach().cpu()]
311        ic_truth_v = [uv_ic[1].detach().cpu()]
312
313        # add IC to power spectrum series
314        prd_coeffs = [dataset.sht(state0[0, plot_channel]).detach().cpu().clone()]
315        ref_coeffs = [prd_coeffs[0].clone()]
316
317        # Initialize history buffer for multi-step models
318        # For n_history=3: buffer = [state0, state0, state0] initially (like AB3's Forward Euler)
319        # Each state is (1, C, H, W), and they get stacked to (1, n_history*C, H, W)
320        if n_history > 1:
321            history_buffer = [state0.clone() for _ in range(n_history)]
322        else:
323            # For 1-step, we rely on 'prd' variable
324            pass # history_buffer = None
325
326        # ML model autoregressive rollout
327        start_time = time.time()
328        # Initialize prd for single-step logic
329        prd = state0 
330        
331        for i in range(1, autoreg_steps + 1):
332            # Build model input based on n_history
333            if n_history > 1:
334                # Stack history: [oldest, ..., newest] -> (1, n_history*C, H, W)
335                model_input = torch.cat(history_buffer, dim=1)
336            else:
337                # Single-step: input is just the previous prediction
338                model_input = prd
339            
340            # Evaluate the ML model
341            prd = model(model_input)  # Output: (1, C, H, W)
342            
343            # Update history buffer (sliding window)
344            if n_history > 1:
345                history_buffer = history_buffer[1:] + [prd.clone()]
346            
347            # Store prediction (only the output C channels, not the stacked input)
348            ic_preds.append(prd[0].detach().cpu())
349            prd_coeffs.append(dataset.sht(prd[0, plot_channel]).detach().cpu().clone())
350            
351            # Calculate and store u/v for prediction
352            # 1. Denormalize to get physical grid values
353            prd_phys = prd[0] * torch.sqrt(inp_var) + inp_mean
354            # 2. Convert to spectral to get vorticity/divergence coeffs
355            prd_spec = dataset.solver.grid2spec(prd_phys)
356            # 3. Calculate u/v from vorticity/divergence (channels 1 and 2)
357            uv_prd = dataset.solver.getuv(prd_spec[1:])
358            ic_preds_u.append(uv_prd[0].detach().cpu())
359            ic_preds_v.append(uv_prd[1].detach().cpu())
360
361        model_times[iic] = time.time() - start_time
362
363        # classical model (or precomputed truth)
364        start_time = time.time()
365        
366        if use_precomputed_truth:
367            # Use precomputed trajectory slice
368            # states_chunk has shape (steps+1, C, H, W)
369            # We already used index 0 for IC. Now iterate 1..steps
370            for i in range(1, autoreg_steps + 1):
371                # Get grid state directly
372                ref_phys = states_chunk[i] # (C, H, W)
373                
374                # Normalize for metrics/loss
375                ref = (ref_phys - inp_mean) / torch.sqrt(inp_var)
376                ic_truth.append(ref.detach().cpu())
377                
378                ref_coeffs.append(dataset.sht(ref[plot_channel]).detach().cpu().clone())
379                
380                # Calculate U/V from physical grid state
381                # Need consistent usage of solver utils
382                ref_spec = dataset.solver.grid2spec(ref_phys)
383                uv_ref = dataset.solver.getuv(ref_spec[1:])
384                ic_truth_u.append(uv_ref[0].detach().cpu())
385                ic_truth_v.append(uv_ref[1].detach().cpu())
386                
387        else:
388            # Run solver on-the-fly
389            for i in range(1, autoreg_steps + 1):
390                # advance classical model
391                uspec = dataset.solver.timestep(uspec, nsteps)
392                ref = (dataset.solver.spec2grid(uspec) - inp_mean) / torch.sqrt(inp_var)
393                
394                # Store truth
395                ic_truth.append(ref.detach().cpu())
396
397                ref_coeffs.append(dataset.sht(ref[plot_channel]).detach().cpu().clone())
398                
399                # Calculate and store u/v for truth
400                # uspec already has physical spectral coefficients
401                uv_ref = dataset.solver.getuv(uspec[1:])
402                ic_truth_u.append(uv_ref[0].detach().cpu())
403                ic_truth_v.append(uv_ref[1].detach().cpu())
404
405        solver_times[iic] = time.time() - start_time
406        
407        # Append to main storage
408        all_preds.append(torch.stack(ic_preds)) # (steps, C, H, W)
409        all_truth.append(torch.stack(ic_truth))
410        all_preds_u.append(torch.stack(ic_preds_u))
411        all_preds_v.append(torch.stack(ic_preds_v))
412        all_truth_u.append(torch.stack(ic_truth_u))
413        all_truth_v.append(torch.stack(ic_truth_v))
414
415        # compute power spectrum and add it to the buffers
416        prd_mean_coeffs.append(torch.stack(prd_coeffs, 0))
417        ref_mean_coeffs.append(torch.stack(ref_coeffs, 0))
418
419        # Compute metrics on the LAST step (as was done originally)
420        # ref re-calculated/extracted for consistency with original loss logic
421        ref_last = ic_truth[-1].to(device).unsqueeze(0) 
422        prd_last = ic_preds[-1].to(device).unsqueeze(0)
423        
424        losses[iic] = loss_fn(prd_last, ref_last)
425        for metric in metrics_fns:
426            metric_buff = metrics[metric]
427            metric_fn = metrics_fns[metric]
428            metric_buff[iic] = metric_fn(prd_last, ref_last)
429
430    # compute the averaged powerspectra 
431    with torch.no_grad():
432        prd_mean_coeffs = torch.stack(prd_mean_coeffs, dim=0).abs().pow(2).mean(dim=0)
433        ref_mean_coeffs = torch.stack(ref_mean_coeffs, dim=0).abs().pow(2).mean(dim=0)
434
435        prd_mean_coeffs[..., 1:] *= 2.0
436        ref_mean_coeffs[..., 1:] *= 2.0
437        prd_mean_ps = prd_mean_coeffs.sum(dim=-1).contiguous()
438        ref_mean_ps = ref_mean_coeffs.sum(dim=-1).contiguous()
439
440    # SAVE NETCDF
441    import xarray as xr
442    
443    # Stack all data: (nics, steps, C, H, W)
444    preds_tensor = torch.stack(all_preds).numpy()
445    truth_tensor = torch.stack(all_truth).numpy()
446    
447    # Stack UV data: (nics, steps, H, W) -> (nics, steps, 1, H, W)
448    preds_u = torch.stack(all_preds_u).unsqueeze(2).numpy()
449    preds_v = torch.stack(all_preds_v).unsqueeze(2).numpy()
450    truth_u = torch.stack(all_truth_u).unsqueeze(2).numpy()
451    truth_v = torch.stack(all_truth_v).unsqueeze(2).numpy()
452    
453    # Concatenate along channel dimension (axis 2)
454    # Resulting shape: (nics, steps, 5, H, W)
455    preds_combined = np.concatenate([preds_tensor, preds_u, preds_v], axis=2)
456    truth_combined = np.concatenate([truth_tensor, truth_u, truth_v], axis=2)
457    
458    # Define coords
459    times = np.arange(autoreg_steps + 1)
460    samples = np.arange(nics)
461    # chans = np.arange(dataset.nlat) if preds_tensor.shape[2] == dataset.nlat else np.arange(preds_tensor.shape[2]) # Usually 3 chans
462    chans = ["geopotential_height", "vorticity", "divergence", "u", "v"]
463    lats = np.linspace(-90, 90, dataset.nlat) # Approx, ideally use dataset.grid
464    lons = np.linspace(0, 360, dataset.nlon, endpoint=False)
465    
466    ds = xr.Dataset(
467        data_vars={
468            "prediction": (("sample", "time", "channel", "lat", "lon"), preds_combined),
469            "truth": (("sample", "time", "channel", "lat", "lon"), truth_combined),
470        },
471        coords={
472            "sample": samples,
473            "time": times,
474            "channel": chans,
475            "lat": lats,
476            "lon": lons,
477        }
478    )
479    
480    scratch_dir = f"/glade/derecho/scratch/{os.environ['USER']}/TH_SWE_output"
481    if not os.path.exists(scratch_dir):
482        os.makedirs(scratch_dir, exist_ok=True)
483    
484    # Extract model name from path_root (.../model_name/figures)
485    model_name = os.path.basename(os.path.dirname(path_root))
486    # Build filename with optional run_tag
487    if run_tag:
488        save_path = os.path.join(scratch_dir, f"{model_name}_{run_tag}_validation_steps{autoreg_steps}.nc")
489    else:
490        save_path = os.path.join(scratch_dir, f"{model_name}_validation_steps{autoreg_steps}.nc")
491    print(f"Saving NetCDF to {save_path}...")
492    ds.to_netcdf(save_path)
493    print(f"Save complete. Output file is located at: {save_path}")
494
495    return losses, metrics, model_times, solver_times
def train_model( model, dataloader, loss_fn, metrics_fns, optimizer, gscaler, scheduler=None, nepochs=20, nfuture=0, num_examples=256, num_valid=0, amp_mode='none', log_grads=0, logging=True, device=device(type='cpu'), n_history=1, noise_injection_sigma=0.0, noise_injection_rate=0.0, plot_callback=None, scratch_dir=None, time_diff2_lambda=0.0): 
 499def train_model(
 500    model,
 501    dataloader,
 502    loss_fn,
 503    metrics_fns,
 504    optimizer,
 505    gscaler,
 506    scheduler=None,
 507    nepochs=20,
 508    nfuture=0,
 509    num_examples=256,
 510    num_valid=0,
 511    amp_mode="none",
 512    log_grads=0,
 513    logging=True,
 514    device=torch.device("cpu"),
 515    n_history=1,  # Number of past timesteps model expects
 516    noise_injection_sigma=0.0,
 517    noise_injection_rate=0.0,
 518    plot_callback=None,
 519    scratch_dir=None,
 520    time_diff2_lambda=0.0,
 521):
 522   
 523    train_start = time.time()
 524
 525    # set AMP type
 526    amp_dtype = torch.float32
 527    if amp_mode == "fp16":
 528        amp_dtype = torch.float16
 529    elif amp_mode == "bf16":
 530        amp_dtype = torch.bfloat16
 531
 532    # count iterations
 533    iters = 0
 534    valid_loss = 0.0 # Initialize valid_loss to prevent UnboundLocalError if nepochs=0
 535    best_valid_loss = float('inf')
 536
 537    for epoch in range(nepochs):
 538
 539        # time each epoch
 540        epoch_start = time.time()
 541
 542        if hasattr(dataloader.dataset, 'set_initial_condition'):
 543            dataloader.dataset.set_initial_condition("random")
 544        if hasattr(dataloader.dataset, 'set_num_examples'):
 545            dataloader.dataset.set_num_examples(num_examples)
 546
 547        # get the solver for its convenience functions
 548        solver = getattr(dataloader.dataset, 'solver', None)
 549
 550        # do the training
 551        accumulated_loss = 0
 552        accumulated_l2_loss = 0
 553        accumulated_lap_loss = 0
 554        accumulated_cons_loss = 0
 555        accumulated_ccc_loss = 0
 556        accumulated_spectral_loss = 0
 557        accumulated_gradient_loss = 0
 558        accumulated_spectral_pv_loss = 0
 559        accumulated_spectral_pv_loss = 0
 560        accumulated_mass_loss = 0
 561        accumulated_time_diff2_loss = 0
 562        model.train()
 563
 564        for batch_idx, (inp, tar) in enumerate(dataloader):
 565
 566            inp, tar = inp.to(device), tar.to(device)
 567
 568            # --- Noise Injection Logic ---
 569            if noise_injection_sigma > 0 and noise_injection_rate > 0 and inp.size(0) >= 2:
 570                batch_size = inp.size(0)
 571                half_batch = batch_size // 2
 572                
 573                # Iterate over the first half of the batch
 574                for i in range(half_batch):
 575                    # With probability `noise_injection_rate`, duplicate sample `i` to `i + half_batch`
 576                    if torch.rand(1).item() < noise_injection_rate:
 577                        target_idx = i + half_batch
 578                        
 579                        # Duplicate input
 580                        inp[target_idx] = inp[i].clone()
 581                        
 582                        # Duplicate target (Ground truth for noisy input is clean target)
 583                        if tar.dim() == 5: # Trajectory (B, T, C, H, W)
 584                             tar[target_idx] = tar[i].clone()
 585                        else: # Single step (B, C, H, W)
 586                             tar[target_idx] = tar[i].clone()
 587                        
 588                        # Add noise to the duplicate input
 589                        noise = torch.randn_like(inp[target_idx]) * noise_injection_sigma
 590                        inp[target_idx] += noise
 591            # -----------------------------
 592
 593            with torch.autocast(device_type="cuda", dtype=amp_dtype, enabled=(amp_mode != "none")):
 594
 595                # Check if tar is a trajectory (5D: B, T, C, H, W) or single target (4D: B, C, H, W)
 596                if tar.dim() == 5:
 597                    # Multi-step trajectory: accumulate loss at each step
 598                    loss = 0
 599                    num_steps = tar.shape[1]
 600                    prev_prev_state = None
 601                    
 602                    if batch_idx == 0 and epoch == 0:
 603                        print(f"DEBUG: Autoregressive training running. tar shape: {tar.shape}, num_steps: {num_steps}, dataset: {dataloader.dataset.__class__.__name__}")
 604                    
 605                    if hasattr(loss_fn, 'reset_stats'):
 606                        loss_fn.reset_stats()
 607                    
 608                    # For multi-history: need to maintain history buffer
 609                    if n_history > 1:
 610                        # inp shape: (B, n_history*C, H, W), extract per-timestep chunks
 611                        B, total_C, H, W = inp.shape
 612                        physical_chans = total_C // n_history  # 3 for SWE
 613                        # Initialize history buffer with input timesteps
 614                        history_buffer = [inp[:, i*physical_chans:(i+1)*physical_chans] for i in range(n_history)]
 615                        
 616                        for step in range(num_steps):
 617                            model_input = torch.cat(history_buffer, dim=1)
 618                            prd = model(model_input)  # Output: (B, C, H, W)
 619                            step_target = tar[:, step]
 620                            
 621                            # Identify prev_target, prev_prev_target for loss
 622                            prev_target = None
 623                            prev_prev_target = None
 624                            
 625                            if step == 0:
 626                                # For step 0, prev_target is last frame of input
 627                                prev_target = history_buffer[-1]
 628                                if n_history > 1:
 629                                     # prev_prev is second to last
 630                                     prev_prev_target = history_buffer[-2]
 631                            else:
 632                                prev_target = tar[:, step-1]
 633                                if step == 1:
 634                                    prev_prev_target = history_buffer[-1]
 635                                else:
 636                                    prev_prev_target = tar[:, step-2]
 637                            
 638                            # For step 0, prev_state is the last input frame (from history or inp)
 639                            # If step > 0, prev_state is the previous prediction
 640                            if step == 0:
 641                                # history_buffer[-1] is the most recent frame
 642                                prev_state = history_buffer[-1]
 643                                if n_history > 1:
 644                                    prev_prev_state = history_buffer[-2]
 645                            else:
 646                                prev_state = history_buffer[-1] 
 647                                if n_history > 1:
 648                                    prev_prev_state = history_buffer[-2]
 649                                else:
 650                                    # For n_history=1, we need to manually track prev_prev if available
 651                                    # But in this branch (n_history>1) we have history_buffer.
 652                                    pass
 653                            
 654                            # Let's simplify: pass model_input (which contains history)
 655                            # CombinedLoss can extract the last frame.
 656                            loss = loss + loss_fn(prd, step_target, 
 657                                                prev_state=prev_state, 
 658                                                prev_prev_state=prev_prev_state,
 659                                                prev_target=prev_target,
 660                                                prev_prev_target=prev_prev_target,
 661                                                step=step)
 662                            
 663                            # Update prev_prev_state for NEXT step (it becomes prev_state of next step)
 664                            if n_history > 1:
 665                                prev_prev_state = history_buffer[-1]
 666                            else:
 667                                prev_prev_state = model_input
 668
 669                            # Update history buffer (sliding window)
 670                            history_buffer = history_buffer[1:] + [prd]
 671                    else:
 672                        # Single-history: original behavior
 673                        prd = inp
 674                        prev_target = None
 675                        prev_prev_target = None
 676                        
 677                        if hasattr(loss_fn, 'reset_stats'):
 678                            loss_fn.reset_stats()
 679                            
 680                        for step in range(num_steps):
 681                            model_input = prd # Save previous state
 682                            
 683                            # Update targets
 684                            step_target = tar[:, step]
 685                            if step == 0:
 686                                prev_target = inp
 687                                prev_prev_target = None # Can't know t-2 easily without history
 688                            else:
 689                                prev_target = tar[:, step-1]
 690                                if step == 1:
 691                                    prev_prev_target = inp
 692                                else:
 693                                    prev_prev_target = tar[:, step-2]
 694
 695                            prd = model(prd)
 696                            
 697                            if step == 0:
 698                                prev_prev_state = None
 699                            else:
 700                                prev_prev_state = model_input
 701                            
 702                            loss = loss + loss_fn(prd, step_target, 
 703                                                prev_state=model_input, 
 704                                                prev_prev_state=prev_prev_state,
 705                                                prev_target=prev_target,
 706                                                prev_prev_target=prev_prev_target,
 707                                                step=step)
 708                    
 709                    # Average over steps for consistent loss magnitude
 710                    loss = loss / num_steps
 711                else:
